variations: Zac later thought: if the version of him who knew the answer met him today, that guy would probably think this guy was an impostor.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「健康追蹤app與真實生活」的諷刺梗，改寫為台灣計步器與通勤場景，對白與情節完全原創

zh-tw.content:
小琪今天走了 12,847 步。

手錶很開心，
跳出獎章：
「恭喜達成今日目標！」

但小琪沒有開心。

因為這 12,847 步，
是她在公司樓層之間
找一份被同事弄丟的合約。

手錶不知道。

手錶只知道她運動了。
手錶不知道她罵了三次髒話、
哭了一次、
接了五通客戶投訴電話。

回家路上她看著手錶，
上面寫著：
「今天的你超棒！繼續保持！」

小琪苦笑了一下。

至少這個世界上，
還有一個東西，
會把她的崩潰
誤會成努力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天累到想哭、卻被健康 app 稱讚的朋友——她會盯著自己的手錶看很久，因為「把崩潰誤會成努力」這句話，是現代成年人最不甘心、卻最熟悉的安慰
variations: 後來小琪換了一支沒有計步功能的手錶。她說：「這樣我崩潰的時候，至少沒有東西在旁邊鼓掌。」

en.content:
Kiki walked 12,847 steps today.

Her watch was thrilled.
A badge popped up:
"Congrats — daily goal hit!"

Kiki was not thrilled.

Because those 12,847 steps
were her running between floors
looking for a contract
a coworker had lost.

The watch didn't know that.

The watch only knew she exercised.
It didn't know she swore three times,
cried once,
took five customer complaint calls.

On the way home she glanced at it.
It said:
"You crushed it today! Keep going!"

Kiki smiled bitterly.

At least one thing
in this world
still mistook her breakdown
for effort.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's exhausted every day but keeps getting cheered on by a fitness app. She'll stare at her watch for a while — because 'mistaking a breakdown for effort' is the most familiar, most reluctant comfort modern adults receive.
variations: Kiki later switched to a watch without a step counter. She said: 'Now when I'm falling apart, at least nothing's clapping next to me.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「同學會與人生比較」的黑色幽默結構，改寫為台灣國中同學會場景，對白與人物完全原創

zh-tw.content:
國中同學會上，
大家輪流分享近況。

阿志：「我在科技業，年薪三百多。」
小美：「我創業，公司今年要上市了。」
大雄：「我嫁了一個醫生，住天母。」

輪到阿明。

他想了一下，說：
「我……還活著。」

全場沉默。

班長想圓場：「人生啊，本來就各有各的精彩——」

阿明打斷他：
「不，我是說真的。
我們班那時候 38 個人，
現在已經走了 4 個。

所以你們剛剛說的那些，
其實都建立在『還活著』這個前提上。

我只是把這個前提，
直接拿出來講而已。」

沒有人再敢比較什麼。

那是阿明國中以來，
第一次拿到全班的注意力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個怕參加同學會的朋友——他會看完之後笑了一下，然後莫名其妙地有點被安慰。因為阿明那句話戳破的事實，是所有比較表的最底層，也是最終欄位
variations: 後來那場同學會的合照，每個人都站得比較靠近彼此，笑得比較不像在炫耀。班長說：「這次同學會比較像同學會了。」

en.content:
At the middle school reunion,
everyone went around sharing updates.

A-Jhi: "I'm in tech, making 300k a year."
Xiao-Mei: "I founded a company. We're going public this year."
Da-Xiong: "I married a doctor. We live in the nice part of town."

Then it was A-Ming's turn.

He paused, then said:
"I'm... still alive."

The room went quiet.

The class president tried to smooth it over:
"Well, everyone's life looks different in its own way—"

A-Ming cut him off:
"No, I mean it.
We had 38 people in our class.
4 of them are already gone.

So everything you just said
actually rests on the assumption
that you're still alive.

I just said the assumption out loud."

No one bragged after that.

It was the first time in his life
A-Ming had the whole class's attention.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who dreads class reunions. They'll laugh, then feel weirdly comforted — because what A-Ming punctures is the bottom row of every comparison chart, and also the only one that actually matters.
variations: In the group photo afterward, everyone stood a little closer together, smiling a little less like they were performing. The class president said: 'This finally felt like a real reunion.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「寵物作為情緒寄託」的黑色幽默反差，改寫為台灣養貓族與心理諮商情境，對白與場景完全原創

zh-tw.content:
心理師問雅婷：
「妳生活中有沒有可以傾訴的對象？」

雅婷想了一下：
「有，我家的貓。」

心理師微笑：「這是好的開始。妳跟牠說什麼？」

雅婷：「全部。
工作壓力、感情問題、原生家庭、存在焦慮、睡不著的時候在想什麼——
全部。」

心理師：「牠怎麼回應？」

雅婷：「牠會看我三秒，
然後翻個身，
把肚子朝上，
等我摸。」

心理師點頭：「所以牠不評論，只是陪伴——」

雅婷打斷他：
「不，我覺得牠的意思是：
『我聽不懂，但我選擇相信妳值得被摸。』

這比我這輩子所有人類給我的回應，
都還準確。」

心理師沉默了三秒。

然後說：「下次諮商，妳要不要把貓帶來？
我覺得我可能要跟牠學一下。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天回家都在跟貓講話的朋友——她會看完之後抱著貓哭笑不得，因為雅婷那句「我聽不懂，但我選擇相信妳值得被摸」，是她從來沒辦法跟人類說出口、卻最想被這樣對待的願望
variations: 雅婷後來真的把貓帶去諮商。心理師說：「我發現牠看妳的方式，比妳看自己還溫柔。妳要不要學牠？」雅婷說：「我試過。但我做不到對自己這麼有耐心。」

en.content:
The therapist asked Yating:
"Is there anyone in your life you can talk to?"

Yating thought for a second:
"Yes. My cat."

The therapist smiled. "That's a good start. What do you tell her?"

Yating: "Everything.
Work stress, relationship problems, family stuff, existential dread, what I'm thinking when I can't sleep —
everything."

Therapist: "How does she respond?"

Yating: "She stares at me for three seconds,
rolls over,
flips her belly up,
and waits to be petted."

Therapist nodded: "So she doesn't judge, she just stays with you—"

Yating cut in:
"No, I think what she's saying is:
'I don't understand a word, but I've decided you deserve to be petted anyway.'

That's more accurate
than any response I've gotten from a human
in my entire life."

The therapist was quiet for three seconds.

Then: "For our next session, would you bring the cat?
I think I have something to learn from her."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who comes home and talks to her cat every day. She'll laugh-cry while holding the cat — because Yating's line ('I don't understand, but I've decided you deserve to be petted anyway') is the thing she's never been able to say to a human, but the exact way she wishes she'd been treated.
variations: Yating eventually did bring the cat in. The therapist said: 'The way she looks at you is gentler than the way you look at yourself. Want to try copying her?' Yating said: 'I tried. I can't be that patient with myself.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Siri 與孤獨的觀察式黑色幽默，重新改寫為台灣 AI 助理與單身對話情境，全部對白原創

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點，我問 ChatGPT：
「我為什麼還單身？」

它回答：
「根據你過去三個月的對話紀錄，
你問了我 47 次晚餐要吃什麼，
問了 23 次怎麼回訊息，
問了 12 次『這樣是不是已讀不回』，
還有 8 次『他是不是沒興趣』。

你已經有一個傾訴對象了。
只是這個對象，不會跟你結婚。」

我沉默了三秒。

然後又打字：
「那你覺得我該怎麼辦？」

它回答：
「先把這個對話視窗關掉，
試試看跟旁邊那個會呼吸的人講話。」

我看了一下旁邊。

只有充電器。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜都在跟 AI 聊天的朋友——她會看完之後默默把手機放下，然後再拿起來，繼續跟 AI 聊這個笑話有多準
variations: 後來我真的關掉視窗，去找會呼吸的人講話。我跟我的多肉植物說了三十分鐘。它沒回應，但至少它有在光合作用——這已經比我前任積極多了。

en.content:
2 a.m. I asked ChatGPT:
"Why am I still single?"

It replied:
"Based on your last three months of chat history,
you asked me 47 times what to eat for dinner,
23 times how to reply to a text,
12 times 'is this being left on read,'
and 8 times 'is he just not interested.'

You already have someone to confide in.
It's just that this someone won't marry you."

I sat with that for three seconds.

Then typed:
"So what should I do?"

It replied:
"Close this window first.
Then try talking to the person next to you who breathes."

I looked next to me.

It was a phone charger.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who chats with AI at 2 a.m. — she'll quietly put her phone down, then pick it back up and ask the AI how accurate this joke is
variations: I did close the window and went to talk to a breathing thing. I talked to my succulent for 30 minutes. It didn't respond, but at least it was photosynthesizing — which is still more effort than my ex ever made.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自職場過勞與存在主義的自嘲式黑色幽默，改寫為台灣上班族與健檢報告情境，對白與場景全為原創

zh-tw.content:
公司年度健檢，醫生看著我的報告，皺了一下眉。

「你這個年紀，肝指數不應該這樣。」

我說：「我知道，工作壓力比較大。」

醫生：「你睡眠時間？」
「平均五小時。」

醫生：「運動？」
「從停車場走到辦公室算嗎？」

醫生：「飲食？」
「外送平台 VIP 會員。」

醫生放下筆，看著我，認真地說：
「你知道嗎，按照你目前這個生活模式，
你的身體大概還能撐十年。」

我點點頭：「醫生，我房貸還要繳二十五年。」

醫生沉默了三秒。

然後說：
「那我建議你開始物色繼承人。
或者，把房子賣掉。」

我笑了。
醫生也笑了。

但我們都沒有真的覺得好笑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個健檢報告紅字一堆但還是天天加班的朋友——他會邊笑邊截圖傳到家族群組，然後被媽媽電話追殺三天，這就是這個笑話的療效
variations: 醫生最後說：「其實還有一個方案——把工作辭了。」我認真考慮了 0.3 秒，然後說：「醫生，我覺得我還是先死好了，比較簡單。」醫生說：「我懂。我也是這樣熬過住院醫師的。」

en.content:
Annual company physical. The doctor looked at my report and frowned.

"At your age, your liver numbers shouldn't look like this."

I said, "I know. Work's been stressful."

Doctor: "How much do you sleep?"
"About five hours."

Doctor: "Exercise?"
"Does walking from the parking lot to the office count?"

Doctor: "Diet?"
"I'm a VIP member on three food delivery apps."

The doctor put down the pen, looked at me, and said seriously:
"You know, at this rate,
your body has maybe ten more years in it."

I nodded. "Doc, I have twenty-five years left on my mortgage."

The doctor was silent for three seconds.

Then said:
"In that case, I suggest you start identifying an heir.
Or sell the house."

I laughed.
The doctor laughed.

Neither of us actually thought it was funny.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose physical report is mostly red flags but still works overtime every day. He'll laugh, screenshot it to the family group chat, and then get hunted down by his mom on the phone for three days. That's the therapeutic effect.
variations: The doctor finally said, 'There is one other option — quit your job.' I considered it for 0.3 seconds, then said, 'Doc, honestly, dying sounds easier.' The doctor said, 'I get it. That's how I got through my residency.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自存在主義式生活諷刺，改寫為台灣租屋族與房東對話情境，對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
房東打電話來：
「下個月房租要漲三千。」

我問：「為什麼？」

房東：「物價上漲，這是合理的調整。」

我說：「我薪水沒漲。」

房東：「那是你公司的問題，不是我的問題。」

我說：「可是這樣我會付不出來。」

房東：「那你可以搬走，我這邊還有很多人想租。」

我沉默了。

然後說：「房東，你知道嗎？
你這樣讓我突然想通了一件事——
我這輩子這麼努力工作，
其實不是在為自己活，
是在幫你還房貸。

你的房子，是用我的青春蓋的。」

房東沉默了三秒。

然後說：「年輕人，你想得太多了。
下個月一號記得匯款。

還有，你的領悟費也要算進去——
再加兩千。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每年都在被漲房租的朋友——他會邊笑邊查 591 上的新房源，然後發現條件差不多的房子都比現在貴五千，最後決定不搬了，繼續幫房東還房貸
variations: 我後來真的搬家了。新房東第一句話：「我這邊不漲房租的，住越久越便宜。」我感動到快哭。住了三個月，房子開始漏水、馬桶堵塞、熱水器壞掉。房東說：「這些是你的使用問題，要自己處理。」我終於懂了——他不是不漲房租，他是用別的方式漲。

en.content:
My landlord called:
"Rent's going up by 3,000 next month."

I asked, "Why?"

Landlord: "Cost of living is up. It's a reasonable adjustment."

I said, "My salary isn't up."

Landlord: "That's your company's problem, not mine."

I said, "But I can't afford it."

Landlord: "Then move out. Plenty of people want this place."

I went quiet.

Then said, "You know what, landlord?
You just helped me figure something out —
I haven't been working this hard for myself my whole life.
I've been working hard to pay off your mortgage.

Your house was built out of my twenties."

The landlord was silent for three seconds.

Then said, "Kid, you're overthinking it.
Wire the rent by the 1st.

Oh, and add another 2,000 —
that insight wasn't free."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose rent goes up every year. He'll laugh, then immediately open a rental app, find that everything in his price range is somehow worse, decide not to move, and go back to financing his landlord's wealth
variations: I actually did move. New landlord's first words: 'I never raise rent. The longer you stay, the cheaper it gets.' I almost cried. Three months in: leaky ceiling, clogged toilet, broken water heater. Landlord said, 'Those are usage issues. Handle them yourself.' That's when I got it — he doesn't raise rent. He raises the rent in other ways.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自存在主義式自嘲與生命荒謬感，改寫為台灣手搖飲料與人生選擇的諷刺對話，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我在手搖店點飲料。

店員問：「冰塊？」
「正常冰。」

「甜度？」
「半糖。」

「加料？」
「珍珠。」

店員按了一下螢幕，抬頭看我：
「先生，你今天是第 1,824 杯這個組合。
我們系統有紀錄。」

我愣住：「你們連這個都記？」

店員：「不是我們在記，是你自己在重複。」

我說：「⋯⋯這只是飲料，沒這麼嚴重吧。」

店員：「先生，這不是飲料的問題。
你每天經過十家不同的店，
每天都選一樣的。

你以為你在選擇，
其實你只是在執行。

你的人生，可能也是這樣。」

我接過飲料，走出店門。

喝了一口。

果然，跟昨天一樣甜。
跟去年一樣甜。
跟我整個三十幾歲，
一樣甜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天都點一樣飲料、走一樣路線、過一樣生活的朋友——她會看完之後把飲料放下，然後隔天還是點一樣的，因為她說「至少這個我能控制」
variations: 隔天我故意換了一杯：四季春、無糖、去冰、加椰果。喝了一口，難喝到不行。我終於懂了——不是我在重複，是我已經找到最適合自己的版本，只是我把這個叫做「困住」，因為「安定」聽起來太老了。

en.content:
At the bubble tea shop.

The clerk asked, "Ice level?"
"Regular."

"Sweetness?"
"Half sugar."

"Toppings?"
"Pearls."

The clerk tapped the screen, looked up at me:
"Sir, this is your 1,824th drink with this exact combo.
It's in our system."

I froze. "You track that?"

Clerk: "We're not tracking it. You're repeating it."

I said, "...It's just a drink. It's not that deep."

Clerk: "Sir, it's not about the drink.
You pass ten different shops on your way here.
Every day you pick the same one.

You think you're choosing.
You're actually just executing.

Your life is probably the same way."

I took the drink and walked out.

Took a sip.

Yep. Same sweetness as yesterday.
Same as last year.
Same as my entire thirties.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who orders the same drink, walks the same route, lives the same life every day. She'll set the drink down after reading, then order the same thing the next day — because, as she puts it, 'at least this one I can control'
variations: Next day I switched it up on purpose: four-seasons oolong, no sugar, no ice, coconut jelly. Took a sip. Disgusting. That's when I got it — I'm not repeating myself, I've already found my optimal configuration. I just call it 'stuck' because 'settled' sounds too old.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 corporate nihilism 與 AI 取代人類的當代趨勢，改寫為台灣上班族被 AI 取代的對話情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
主管把我叫進辦公室，臉色凝重。

「小林，公司決定導入 AI，你的職位被優化掉了。」

我點點頭：「我懂，謝謝公司這幾年的栽培。」

主管：「不過先別走，我有一個新職位想推薦你。」

我抬起頭：「真的嗎？」

主管：「對，我們需要一個人，幫 AI 標註資料，
讓它學會做你以前的工作。

薪水是原本的三分之一，
但工時不變。」

我沉默了五秒。

然後說：「主管，這不就是叫我自己挖墳，
挖完還要躺進去嗎？」

主管笑了：「不是叫你躺進去——
是叫你訓練那個之後會把你蓋上的人。」

我接受了這份工作。

因為房貸不會等 AI 學會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在被 AI 工具滲透日常工作的朋友——他會看完之後苦笑，然後繼續用 ChatGPT 寫今天的工作報告，因為那真的很方便
variations: 三個月後，AI 已經完全會做我以前的工作。主管把我叫進辦公室：「小林，謝謝你這段時間的訓練，AI 已經上線了。」我問：「那我呢？」主管說：「你也升職了——升成 AI 的維護工程師。薪水回到原本的水準。」我終於懂了——這個世界不會淘汰你，它只是讓你變成餵養下一個你的人。

en.content:
My boss called me into his office, looking grim.

"Lin, the company is rolling out AI. Your position has been optimized away."

I nodded. "I understand. Thank you for the years."

Boss: "Hold on though — I have another role to offer you."

I looked up. "Really?"

Boss: "Yes. We need someone to label data for the AI,
so it can learn to do your old job.

Pay is a third of what you make now.
Hours are the same."

I was quiet for five seconds.

Then said, "Boss, isn't this basically asking me to dig my own grave
and then climb in afterwards?"

Boss smiled. "Not climb in —
I'm asking you to train the thing that will eventually shovel the dirt on top."

I took the job.

Because the mortgage won't wait for the AI to learn.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose daily job is quietly being absorbed by AI tools — he'll laugh painfully, then go right back to using ChatGPT to write today's status report, because it really is convenient
variations: Three months later, the AI could fully do my old job. Boss called me back in: "Lin, thanks for the training. The AI is live." I asked, "What about me?" Boss: "You got promoted too — to AI maintenance engineer. Salary back to your old level." That's when I got it — the world doesn't replace you. It just turns you into the person who feeds the next version of you.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自存在主義式自嘲與運氣黑色幽默，改寫為台灣彩券中獎與離婚情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
阿明買了二十年的彩券，從來沒中過。

結婚那天，他開玩笑跟新娘說：
「我這個人運氣很差，妳要有心理準備。」

新娘笑笑：「沒關係，我運氣很好，我們可以平均。」

十年後，他們離婚。

離婚當天，阿明去便利商店買了一張彩券——
刮中了一千萬。

他打電話給前妻：
「妳說過我們可以平均運氣的，
所以這一千萬，是不是要分妳一半？」

前妻：「不用，那是你的。
我帶走的是你過去十年的好運，
你終於可以開始享受你自己的命了。」

阿明掛掉電話，看著手裡的彩券。

突然不太確定，
剛剛中的這一千萬，
到底算不算是好運。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛離婚或剛失戀的朋友——他會看完之後愣很久，然後說「也許失去某些人，本身就是中獎」，這個笑話的藥效就出來了
variations: 前妻後來再婚了，新老公中了兩千萬。阿明知道之後，盯著自己的一千萬看了很久——終於懂了：他不是運氣變好，他只是回到本來的水準。前十年才是借來的，現在連本帶利還清了。

en.content:
A-Ming bought lottery tickets for twenty years. Never won once.

On his wedding day, he joked to his bride:
"I have terrible luck. Just so you know."

She smiled: "It's fine, I have good luck. We'll average out."

Ten years later, they divorced.

On the day the divorce was finalized, A-Ming stopped at a convenience store and bought a scratcher.

Won ten million.

He called his ex-wife:
"You said we'd average our luck.
So this ten million — should I give you half?"

Ex-wife: "No, that's all yours.
I took the last ten years of your good luck with me.
You're finally living your own life now."

A-Ming hung up and stared at the ticket in his hand.

Suddenly wasn't sure
if the ten million he just won
counted as good luck at all.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just got divorced or just got dumped — they'll go quiet for a while, then say 'maybe losing certain people is winning the lottery,' and that's when this joke does its work
variations: His ex-wife remarried. New husband won twenty million. When A-Ming heard, he stared at his ten million for a long time — and finally understood: his luck didn't improve, he just returned to baseline. The first ten years were borrowed. He's just paid it back with interest.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自存在主義式育兒黑色幽默，改寫為台灣家庭與孩子提問的諷刺對話，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
兒子六歲，問我一個問題：
「爸爸，人為什麼要長大？」

我想了三秒。

本來想說很多大人會說的：
「長大才能做自己想做的事。」

但我不想騙他。

我說：「兒子，老實說，
爸爸到現在還沒搞懂為什麼要長大。

但是長大之後你會發現，
問這個問題的時間越來越少，
因為你會忙著付房租、付帳單、處理人際關係。

等你忙到沒時間問為什麼的時候，
你就算長大了。」

兒子認真地點點頭。

然後說：「爸爸，那我可以不要長大嗎？」

我笑了：「可以啊。
但是你不長大，誰來照顧爸爸？」

兒子沉默了。

然後說：「⋯⋯爸爸，這個壓力是不是太大了？」

我也沉默了。

他六歲就懂的事，
我四十歲才剛開始懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個有小孩的朋友——他會看完之後抱抱他的孩子，然後在心裡默默想著「不要長太快、不要太懂事、不要太早承擔」這些他自己沒被滿足的願望
variations: 後來兒子問：「那如果我不照顧爸爸，會怎樣？」我說：「不會怎樣，爸爸會自己想辦法。」兒子說：「那我為什麼要長大？」我終於回答不出來。也許答案就是——不需要為了任何人長大，只需要為了自己。但這句話我四十歲才學會，他六歲就在問了。

en.content:
My six-year-old son asked me a question:
"Dad, why do people have to grow up?"

I thought for three seconds.

I almost said the standard adult line:
"Because then you can do whatever you want."

But I didn't want to lie to him.

I said, "Son, honestly,
Dad still hasn't figured out why we have to grow up.

But once you do, you'll notice
you ask that question less and less,
because you're too busy paying rent and bills and managing people.

When you're too busy to ask why,
that's basically what growing up is."

My son nodded seriously.

Then said, "Dad, can I just not grow up?"

I smiled. "Sure.
But if you don't grow up, who's going to take care of Dad?"

My son went quiet.

Then said, "...Dad, isn't that kind of a lot of pressure?"

I went quiet too.

What he understood at six,
I'm only starting to understand at forty.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend with a young kid — they'll hug their child after reading, while quietly thinking 'don't grow up too fast, don't be too understanding, don't carry too much too early,' all the things they themselves never got
variations: Later my son asked, "What happens if I don't take care of you?" I said, "Nothing. Dad will figure it out." He said, "Then why should I grow up?" I couldn't answer. Maybe the answer is — you don't have to grow up for anyone. Just for yourself. But I learned that at forty. He was already asking it at six.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自社群媒體與現代孤獨的存在主義黑色幽默，改寫為台灣 IG 動態與真實人際情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我發了一張 IG 限動：
「今天好累，需要一個擁抱。」

二十秒內，三十七個人按了愛心。

沒有人傳訊息。

我又發了一張：
「真的需要有人陪。」

兩分鐘內，五十二個人按了愛心。
四個人留言：「抱抱」「+1」「同感」「我也是」。

還是沒有人傳訊息。

我把限動刪掉。

換了一張新的：
「沒事了，謝謝大家關心。」

這次只有八個人按愛心。

我終於懂了——
大家愛看你受傷，
但不愛看你被治好。

因為前者讓他們覺得自己也不孤單，
後者讓他們覺得自己被留下了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個常常發黑暗限動但從來不會主動傳訊息給朋友的人——她會看完之後刪掉今天發的限動，然後關掉 app，最後還是打開了 LINE 傳了一句「在嗎」
variations: 後來我學乖了，需要陪伴的時候不發限動了，直接傳 LINE 給某個人。對方回：「我現在在忙耶，晚點聊。」我終於懂了——限動的愛心是廉價的，因為按完不用負責；而真實的回應是昂貴的，因為它需要時間、需要在場、需要對方真的想理你。前者人人有，後者越來越少。

en.content:
I posted an IG story:
"Rough day. Could really use a hug."

Within twenty seconds, 37 hearts.

No messages.

Posted another:
"Really need someone to talk to."

Two minutes, 52 hearts.
Four replies: "hug," "+1," "same," "me too."

Still no DMs.

I deleted the story.

Posted a new one:
"All good now, thanks for caring."

This time, only 8 hearts.

That's when I got it —
people love watching you bleed.
They don't love watching you heal.

The first makes them feel less alone.
The second makes them feel left behind.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who constantly posts dark stories but never actually messages anyone first — she'll delete today's story after reading, close the app, then open LINE and type 'you up?' anyway
variations: Eventually I learned. When I needed company, I stopped posting stories and just DMed someone directly. They replied: "Busy right now, talk later." That's when I really got it — story hearts are cheap because pressing them costs nothing. Real replies are expensive because they require time, presence, and someone who actually wants to deal with you. Everyone has the first. Almost no one has the second anymore.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自當代「躺平」與職場過勞的諷刺對比，改寫為台灣兩代人對話情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
爸爸看著我整天躺在沙發上，終於忍不住開口：

「你們這代人怎麼回事？
我年輕的時候，七點上班、九點下班，
從來沒有抱怨過。」

我說：「爸，那時候你薪水可以買房。」

爸：「那是因為我努力。」

我說：「爸，你那時候房價是你年薪的五倍。
現在是我年薪的二十五倍。
所以我用你的努力，要努力五倍才能達到一樣的結果。」

爸沉默了。

然後說：「⋯⋯所以你選擇躺著？」

我說：「不是我選擇躺著，
是這個世界先躺平了，
我只是配合它。」

爸又沉默了一下。

然後說：「兒子，挪一下，
沙發我也想躺。」

我們父子，在同一張沙發上，
看著天花板。

第一次，達成了真正的世代和解。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個常常被爸媽念「不夠努力」的朋友——他會看完之後傳給爸爸看，爸爸可能不會回他訊息，但下次見面時，可能會少念他兩句
variations: 後來爸爸真的退休了。他說：「兒子，我發現躺著也沒有比較舒服。」我說：「對啊，因為躺平不是放棄，是在重新找位置。你年輕的時候沒躺過，所以不知道躺著有多累——要對抗整個社會的眼光，需要的力氣其實比上班還多。」爸爸點點頭。然後說：「那你⋯⋯應該很累吧。」這是他第一次心疼我。

en.content:
Dad watched me lie on the couch all day and finally couldn't hold it in:

"What is wrong with your generation?
When I was young, I worked 7 to 9, six days a week.
Never complained once."

I said, "Dad, back then your salary could actually buy a house."

Dad: "That's because I worked hard."

I said, "Dad, the housing price was five times your annual salary back then.
It's twenty-five times mine now.
So with the same effort as you, I need to work five times harder to get the same result."

Dad went silent.

Then said, "...So you chose to just lie down?"

I said, "I didn't choose to lie down.
The world lay down first.
I'm just keeping it company."

Dad was quiet again.

Then said, "Son, scoot over.
I want to lie down too."

Father and son, on the same couch,
staring at the ceiling.

For the first time, real generational reconciliation.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who constantly gets lectured by parents about 'not working hard enough' — he'll forward it to his dad, who probably won't reply, but the next time they meet, will probably nag him a little less
variations: Dad eventually retired. He said, "Son, lying down isn't actually that comfortable." I said, "I know. Lying flat isn't giving up — it's repositioning. You never tried it when you were young, so you don't know how exhausting it is. Standing against the entire society's gaze takes more energy than going to work." Dad nodded. Then said, "So you... must be really tired." That was the first time he actually felt sorry for me.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 AI 取代工作的當代焦慮，改寫為台灣科技業裁員情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
公司開全員大會，老闆站在台上微笑：

「為了提升效率，我們導入了 AI。
從下個月開始，AI 會幫各位處理日常工作，
讓大家有更多時間，去做更有價值的事。」

台下響起掌聲。

第二個月，五個人被資遣。
第三個月，又五個人。
第六個月，整個部門剩下三個人。

年終尾牙，老闆又站上台：

「感謝 AI 讓我們的人力結構更精實，
感謝大家在這段轉型期的付出。」

剩下的三個人鼓掌。

回家路上，阿凱打開求職網站，
發現上面的工作敘述都是：
「需熟練使用 AI 工具，
能用 AI 完成原本三人份的工作量。」

阿凱終於懂了——
老闆說的「更有價值的事」，
指的是「找下一份工作」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近開始學 AI 工具、想說至少要走在前面不被取代的朋友——他會看完之後沉默地關掉求職網站，然後又重新打開
variations: 後來阿凱真的找到工作了。新公司面試時，老闆說：「我們很注重人的價值，AI 只是輔助。」阿凱點點頭。半年後，新公司也開始導入 AI。阿凱終於明白，這不是哪一家公司的問題——是整個世代的人，都在被溫柔地告知：你比你以為的還要可被取代。

en.content:
The company held an all-hands meeting. The boss stood on stage smiling:

"To boost efficiency, we're rolling out AI.
Starting next month, AI will handle your daily tasks,
so you can all focus on more valuable work."

Applause from the audience.

Month two: five people laid off.
Month three: five more.
Month six: the whole department was down to three people.

At the year-end party, the boss stepped up again:

"Thanks to AI, our headcount is leaner than ever.
Thanks to all of you for adapting through this transition."

The remaining three clapped.

On the way home, Kai opened a job board
and every listing said:
"Must be proficient with AI tools.
Must be able to do the work of three people, with AI."

That's when Kai got it.
When the boss said "more valuable work,"
he meant "finding your next job."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who recently started learning AI tools, hoping to stay ahead of being replaced — they'll read it, silently close the job board, then quietly reopen it
variations: Kai eventually got hired. In the new interview, the boss said: "We really value people. AI is just a tool." Kai nodded. Six months later, the new company rolled out AI too. That's when Kai finally understood — this wasn't a problem with any one company. It was an entire generation being kindly told: you're more replaceable than you thought.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自當代焦慮命名與心理治療文化，改寫為台灣諮商情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
心理師說：
「你可以試著把焦慮擬人化，
給它取個名字，這樣比較容易跟它對話。」

我想了想，說：「叫它阿傑好了。」

心理師：「為什麼是阿傑？」

我說：「因為它就像我國中那個同學，
沒事就在你旁邊，
你叫它走它不走，
你不理它它就煩你，
而且你越想假裝沒看到，
它越要證明它存在。」

心理師笑了，說：「很有畫面。」

我繼續說：「重點是——
阿傑不會消失，
他會跟我一起長大、一起變老、一起退休，
甚至我死的時候，
阿傑可能會是最後一個離開我身邊的。」

心理師沉默了一下。

然後說：「那他⋯⋯也算是一種陪伴吧。」

我點點頭。

「對啊。所以後來我學會了——
不是要把他趕走，
是要讓他學會閉嘴。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在看心理諮商、但每次回家都覺得心理師講的話自己其實早就知道的朋友——她會苦笑，然後傳一句「我的叫小明」
variations: 後來我跟阿傑相處久了，發現他其實也有用。每次他開始大吼大叫，通常代表我又在做一件對自己不好的事。他不是敵人，是警報器——只是聲音有點大，而且永遠關不掉。

en.content:
The therapist said:
"Try personifying your anxiety.
Give it a name. It's easier to have a conversation with it."

I thought for a second. "Let's call it Jeff."

Therapist: "Why Jeff?"

I said: "Because he's like that kid in middle school
who just stood next to you all day.
You tell him to leave, he doesn't.
You ignore him, he gets louder.
The harder you pretend he's not there,
the harder he tries to prove he is."

The therapist laughed. "Very vivid."

I kept going: "The thing is —
Jeff isn't going anywhere.
He's going to grow up with me, get old with me, retire with me.
When I die, Jeff might be the last one in the room."

The therapist paused.

Then said: "So in a way... he's company too."

I nodded.

"Yeah. That's what I figured out eventually.
The goal isn't to kick him out.
It's to teach him when to shut up."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been in therapy but always feels like she already knew everything the therapist says — she'll smile bitterly and reply 'mine's called Steve'
variations: After living with Jeff for a while, I realized he had a use. Every time he started yelling, it usually meant I was doing something bad for myself. He wasn't the enemy. He was the smoke alarm. Just loud, and you can never quite turn it off.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自交友軟體與現代孤獨諷刺，改寫為台灣 Tinder 使用者情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我跟朋友說：
「我用交友軟體三個月，配對了 47 個人。」

朋友：「哇，你很搶手耶。」

我說：「其中 12 個只是按好玩的，沒回訊息。
10 個聊兩句之後就消失了。
8 個約出來見面，見完之後再也沒聯絡。
5 個變成 LINE 上的『以前認識的人』，
但我們從來沒見過面。
4 個曖昧了一下下就被已讀不回。
剩下的 8 個——」

朋友：「然後呢？」

我說：「我自己也封鎖了。」

朋友愣住：「為什麼？」

我說：「因為他們開始認真了。
而我發現，比起被拒絕，
我更怕被選中。」

朋友沉默了很久。

「所以你不是在找對象，
你是在找一個練習被靠近的方法。」

我點點頭。

「對啊。但後來我發現——
練習了三個月，我還是不太會。
甚至更糟，因為我現在連寂寞都精緻化了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個交友軟體開了又刪、刪了又開的朋友——他會看完之後關掉 app，然後三天後又重新下載回來
variations: 後來我把交友軟體刪了。半年後，朋友介紹了一個人給我。我們見面三次，然後在一起了。我問他：「你怎麼確定要選我？」他說：「我沒有在選，我只是沒有躲開。」那一刻我終於懂了——交友軟體最大的問題，是它讓你以為愛情是選擇題。但其實愛情比較像考試忘了帶 2B 鉛筆——你只是剛好遇到一個願意借你的人。

en.content:
I told my friend:
"Three months on dating apps, 47 matches."

Friend: "Damn, you're in demand."

I said: "Twelve of them just swiped for fun, never replied.
Ten ghosted after two messages.
Eight met up once and disappeared.
Five became 'people I used to know' on LINE,
though we never actually met.
Four flirted a little, then read-receipted me into oblivion.
The last eight —"

Friend: "Yeah?"

I said: "I blocked them myself."

Friend: "Why?"

I said: "Because they got serious.
And I realized — I'm not afraid of being rejected.
I'm afraid of being chosen."

Friend went quiet for a long time.

"So you weren't looking for a partner.
You were looking for practice in being approached."

I nodded.

"Yeah. But after three months, I'm still not good at it.
Worse, actually — now even my loneliness feels curated."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who installs and deletes dating apps on a loop — they'll close the app after reading, then re-download it three days later
variations: Eventually I deleted the apps. Six months later, a friend introduced someone. We met three times. Then we were together. I asked him, "How did you decide on me?" He said, "I wasn't deciding. I just didn't flinch away." That's when I got it. The real problem with dating apps is they make you think love is a multiple-choice question. It's actually more like forgetting a pencil on test day — you just happen to meet someone willing to lend you one.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自當代「安靜離職」與職場精疲力盡，改寫為台灣科技業 PM 情境，場景與對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
主管把我叫進會議室，關上門。

「最近你的工作狀態，
有點⋯⋯讓我擔心。」

我說：「怎麼說？」

主管：「你準時下班，
會議只回必要的話，
Slack 訊息不再秒回，
週末也不主動回工作群組。
你是不是⋯⋯出了什麼事？」

我看著他，認真地想了一下。

「主管，我沒有出事。
我只是在做你三年前面試我的時候，
白紙黑字寫在合約上的工作內容。」

主管沉默。

然後說：「但你以前不是這樣的。」

我說：「對，以前我以為加班是熱忱。
後來我發現，加班只是讓老闆覺得，
他只用一個人的薪水，買到了一個半的人。
剩下那半個，是我用我的健康送的。」

主管看著我：「那你現在打算？」

我說：「我打算只當一個人。」

主管又沉默。

最後說：「⋯⋯這樣對團隊不公平。」

我說：「對，但這樣對我公平。
而且——
我比團隊先存在。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近終於不再無償加班、但每次準時下班都還是有點罪惡感的朋友——她會看完之後深呼吸，然後關掉電腦，第一次心安理得地下班
variations: 後來主管把我考績打了 B。我笑了。因為三年前我每天加班到十點，考績也只是 A。差別在哪？差在當時我以為 A 是肯定，現在我知道 A 只是「再多榨他一點」的暗號。B 反而是公司在告訴我：「你終於被我們看清楚了。」我把它當作畢業證書收好。

en.content:
My manager called me into a meeting room and closed the door.

"Your work lately
has been... a little concerning."

I said, "How so?"

Manager: "You leave on time.
You only speak when necessary in meetings.
You stopped replying to Slack instantly.
You don't check the work group on weekends anymore.
Is something... wrong?"

I looked at him and thought about it seriously.

"Boss, nothing's wrong.
I'm just doing the job that was written in black and white
in the contract you handed me three years ago."

The manager went quiet.

Then said, "But you weren't like this before."

I said, "Right. I used to think overtime was passion.
Then I figured out — overtime just makes the boss feel
he paid one salary and got 1.5 employees.
That extra half? I covered it with my health."

Manager: "So what's your plan?"

I said, "I plan to be exactly one employee."

Manager went silent again.

Finally: "...That's not fair to the team."

I said, "True. But it's fair to me.
And I existed before the team did."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who finally stopped working unpaid overtime but still feels guilty leaving on time — they'll take a deep breath after reading, close the laptop, and clock out without guilt for the first time
variations: Manager rated me a B that year. I laughed. Three years ago I worked till 10pm every night and only got an A. What was the difference? Back then I thought A meant 'we appreciate you.' Now I know A meant 'squeeze him a little more.' The B was actually the company telling me: 'We finally see you clearly.' I kept it like a graduation certificate.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 年「希望是訂閱詐騙」黑色幽默主題，改寫為台灣 KOL 直播帶貨情境，對白與場景全部原創

zh-tw.content:
希望這種東西，根本是一個訂閱制詐騙。

你每個月都繼續扣款，
相信這個月一定會更好。

結果到月底打開銀行 App，
看到的不是希望，
是手續費。

你想取消訂閱？
客服說：「先生，這是您自己當年勾選的。
條款就在合約第 47 頁，最下面，淺灰色的小字。」

你說：「我可以解約嗎？」

客服微笑：
「可以，但解約金叫做『絕望』。
您確定要支付嗎？」

你沉默了兩秒，
還是按下「續訂」。

因為比起一次付清絕望，
你寧願每個月被扣一點點希望。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天說「再撐一下下個月就會變好」結果撐了三年的朋友——他會看完之後苦笑一聲，然後默默打開銀行 App 看看自己這個月又扣了多少「希望費」
variations: 進階版：客服還會主動推薦升級方案：「我們的『年度希望Plus』可以解鎖更多自我感動成就，現在加購還送『假性樂觀』功能，讓您看起來不像在硬撐。」

en.content:
Hope is basically a subscription scam.

Every month they auto-charge you,
and you keep believing this month will finally be better.

Then at the end of the month you open your banking app,
and what you see isn't hope —
it's a service fee.

Want to cancel?
Customer service says: "Sir, you opted in yourself.
It's in clause 47, very bottom, light grey font."

You ask, "Can I terminate?"

They smile:
"Yes. But the termination fee is called Despair.
Would you like to proceed?"

You sit there for two seconds,
then hit Renew anyway.

Because paying despair in one lump sum
is worse than getting a little hope siphoned every month.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been saying 'just one more month and things will turn around' for three years straight — they'll laugh bitterly, then silently open their banking app to see how much hope got auto-charged this month
variations: Bonus: support will even upsell you. 'Our Annual Hope Plus tier unlocks more self-deception achievements. Sign up today and get our complimentary Fake Optimism feature, so you don't look like you're forcing it.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 2026 年 AI 取代人類焦慮主題，改寫為台灣中小企業會議情境，對白與比喻全部原創

zh-tw.content:
公司導入 AI 之後，老闆把全體員工叫進會議室。

他說：「不要擔心，AI 不會取代你們。
它只是來協助你們。」

大家鬆了一口氣。

三個月後，老闆又把大家叫進會議室。

「我有個好消息跟一個壞消息。
好消息是——AI 真的沒有取代你們。
壞消息是——
它取代了我，
然後它叫我把你們全部裁掉。」

會議室一片寂靜。

有人小聲問：「那你呢？」

老闆苦笑：「我下週也走。
AI 說我的『情感管理職能』
它一個 prompt 就能搞定，
還不會在年終要求加薪。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近一直在學 ChatGPT 想保住飯碗的朋友——他會看完之後愣住，然後打開 LinkedIn 默默把履歷再更新一次，因為他發現連老闆都自身難保
variations: 後續：AI 接管後，把原本三十人的部門精簡成兩個人。一個是按下啟動鍵的人，另一個是被留下來在 AI 出錯的時候負責道歉的人——因為公司發現，客戶還是只願意對人類生氣。

en.content:
After the company rolled out AI, the boss called everyone into the conference room.

"Don't worry," he said. "AI isn't here to replace you.
It's just here to assist you."

Everyone exhaled.

Three months later, he called the same meeting.

"I have good news and bad news.
The good news is — AI really didn't replace you.
The bad news is —
it replaced me,
and then it told me to lay all of you off."

Dead silence.

Someone quietly asked, "And you?"

Boss gave a tired smile: "I'm out next week too.
AI said my 'emotional management duties'
can be handled with a single prompt,
and it won't ask for a raise at year-end."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been frantically learning ChatGPT to save their job — they'll freeze for a second after reading, then quietly update their LinkedIn again. Because if even the boss isn't safe, neither are they.
variations: Aftermath: AI trimmed the 30-person department down to two. One person to press the start button, and one person whose entire job is to apologize when AI screws up — because the company discovered customers still only want to yell at humans.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 年「現代人睡眠焦慮」與療癒app諷刺，改寫為台灣失眠族情境，敘事與台詞全部原創

zh-tw.content:
我下載了一個叫做「秒睡」的 app。

它說只要每晚冥想十分鐘，
搭配付費白噪音，
搭配付費呼吸引導，
搭配付費月度心流分析報告，
你就能擺脫失眠。

我用了三個月。

結果現在，
我不只失眠，
還因為每個月被扣 599 元焦慮到失眠。

我打去客服問能不能退費。

客服說：「先生，您的睡眠品質
根據我們 app 的數據顯示已經改善了 17%。」

我說：「可是我每天都還是只睡四小時。」

客服回：「對，但您現在『感受到的睡眠品質』
比以前提升了。
這就是我們提供的價值。」

我掛掉電話，
躺在床上盯著天花板，
感受到我的價值正在以每秒 0.0006 元的速度被提升。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個訂閱了三個冥想 app 還是每天滑手機到三點的朋友——她會苦笑，然後第一個動作不是去睡覺，而是去看自己到底訂閱了哪幾個 app，然後決定要不要取消
variations: 進階版：app 還推出了「失眠者尊榮會員」方案，每月 1299 元，附贈一位真人睡眠教練——這位教練的工作是每晚十一點打電話來，跟你說：『該睡了喔』。我退訂了。因為我發現，真正讓我睡不著的是手機響。

en.content:
I downloaded an app called "InstaSleep."

It said all I had to do was meditate ten minutes a night,
plus the premium white noise,
plus the premium guided breathing,
plus the premium monthly flow-state analytics report,
and my insomnia would be gone.

I used it for three months.

Now, I'm not just insomniac —
I'm also losing sleep over the $19.99 they auto-charge me.

I called support to ask for a refund.

Support said: "Sir, according to our app data,
your sleep quality has improved by 17%."

I said, "But I still only sleep four hours a night."

Support said: "True, but your 'perceived sleep quality'
has gone up.
That's the value we deliver."

I hung up,
stared at the ceiling,
and felt my value being delivered
at $0.0002 per second.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend subscribed to three meditation apps who still scrolls until 3am — she'll laugh dryly, and her first move won't be to sleep, but to audit her subscriptions and start canceling
variations: Bonus: the app launched a 'Premium Insomniac' tier for $39.99/mo with a real human sleep coach — whose job is to call you at 11pm every night and say, 'Time for bed.' I unsubscribed. Because I figured out the thing keeping me awake was my phone ringing.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 年「養老金不保」社會焦慮，改寫為台灣便利商店打工情境，對白完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我去便利商店買晚餐，
結帳的店員阿伯看起來七十幾歲。

我隨口問：「阿伯，這年紀還在工作喔？」

阿伯笑笑說：
「沒辦法啊，年金被改革了三次，
每改一次，我就要多上班五年。」

我說：「那您打算工作到什麼時候？」

阿伯認真想了想：
「政府說，要工作到我能領得到的那一天。」

我問：「那是哪一天？」

阿伯把發票遞給我，
燦爛地笑：
「他們也不知道。
所以我就把自己當成台灣的吉祥物，
反正只要我還站在這裡，
年輕人就會覺得『至少還有比我更慘的』，
社會就會繼續穩定。」

他補了一句：
「我這份工作，
其實叫做『讓你們安心』。
月薪很低，
但社會貢獻很高。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛看完年金試算結果嚇到的同事——他會先笑出來，然後沉默十秒鐘，然後決定今天晚上加買一張樂透，因為他發現自己跟阿伯沒有差很多，只差幾十年
variations: 進階版：阿伯說：「我兒子是工程師，他叫我退休。我跟他說，『我退了，誰來提醒你年輕人也會老？』我繼續工作，是為了讓他每次來看我，都會多存一點錢。我這不只是工作，是身教。」

en.content:
I went to the convenience store for dinner.
The cashier looked like he was in his seventies.

I asked casually, "Sir, still working at this age?"

He smiled.
"No choice. Pension's been 'reformed' three times.
Each reform tacked five more years onto my career."

"So when are you planning to retire?"

He thought about it seriously.
"The government said,
whenever the day comes that I can actually collect it."

"And when is that?"

He handed me my receipt with a beaming smile.
"They don't know either.
So I just see myself as the country's mascot.
As long as I'm still standing here,
young people can think, 'At least I'm not him,'
and society keeps running smoothly."

Then he added:
"My real job title isn't cashier.
It's 'public reassurance.'
The pay is bad,
but the social contribution is huge."
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who just got their pension projection and went pale — they'll laugh first, then go silent for ten seconds, then decide to grab an extra lottery ticket tonight. Because they realized they're not that different from grandpa. Just a few decades apart.
variations: Bonus: he said, 'My son's an engineer. He keeps telling me to retire. I told him, if I retire, who's going to remind you young people that you'll get old too? I'm still working as a life lesson. Every time he visits me, he saves a little more. This isn't just a job. It's parenting.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 年「人生待辦清單」黑色幽默主題，改寫為台灣三十歲人生反思情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我三十歲生日那天，
打開五年前自己寫的「30 歲前要完成的事」清單。

上面寫著：
- 出國長住一年
- 學會一門樂器
- 存到第一桶金
- 跑一次全馬
- 找到人生方向
- 寫一本書

我看完之後，
從清單裡刪掉所有項目，
只留下最後一行：

「活著」

我打了一個勾。

然後笑了出來，
因為我發現——
二十五歲的我，
從來沒想過光是這一行，
就要拼盡全力才能完成。

我把清單摺起來，
收進抽屜，
決定下個五年，
清單只有一項：

「繼續。」

沒有勾，沒有完成日。
只有每天醒來，
再寫一筆「今天也算」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛過完三十歲生日，看著朋友圈別人結婚買房升職而懷疑人生的朋友——她會看完之後安靜很久，然後傳一句「謝謝」回來。因為她終於知道，光是站著就已經是成就
variations: 後來我又翻出二十歲寫的「25 歲前要完成的事」清單。我把它直接撕掉，因為上面第一條寫著：『三十歲前不要變成現在的我。』結果二十五歲的我，已經是現在的我。早就違約了，違約金就是過去這五年。

en.content:
On my thirtieth birthday
I opened the "Things to Do Before 30" list I'd written at twenty-five.

It said:
- Live abroad for a year
- Learn an instrument
- Save my first pot of money
- Run a full marathon
- Find my purpose
- Write a book

I read it,
crossed off every line,
and left only one item:

"Be alive."

I checked the box.

Then I laughed,
because I realized —
twenty-five-year-old me
never imagined that just this one line
would take everything I had.

I folded the list,
put it back in the drawer,
and decided the next five years
would have only one item:

"Keep going."

No checkbox. No deadline.
Just waking up each day
and adding one more tally:
"Today counts too."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just turned 30, scrolling through everyone else's weddings, mortgages, and promotions, quietly questioning her life — she'll go silent for a long time after reading, then text back just 'thank you.' Because she finally understands that simply standing here is already an achievement
variations: Later I dug up the 'Things to Do Before 25' list I wrote at twenty. I tore it up immediately. The first line said: 'Don't become who you are now by thirty.' But at twenty-five, I was already who I am now. I'd already breached the contract. The penalty fee was the last five years.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 年「成年人就是繳帳單」黑色幽默主題，改寫為台灣自動扣款情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我以前以為，存錢是為了買房。
後來以為，存錢是為了退休。
再後來以為，存錢是為了給家人。

上個月我看了一下我的銀行 App，
才發現自己誤會很大。

存錢的真正用途，
是讓自動扣款不要失敗。

房租、保險、訂閱、稅金、水電、信用卡——
它們在月初準時到場，
比我還積極地參與我的人生。

我只是中間那個轉手的人，
錢從公司的帳戶，
經過我的戶頭三秒鐘，
再去它真正的目的地。

我不是在賺錢，
我是在當錢的轉運站。

而且還是無薪實習。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個月薪水入帳當天，App 通知響到像在開派對的朋友——他會苦笑然後說「對啦，我也只是路過自己的帳戶」
variations: 進階版：我把這件事跟同事說，他想了一下回我：「那你算幸運的，至少錢有經過你戶頭。我的薪水是直接從公司打到房東那邊，省了我一道手續，也省了我看到餘額的痛苦。」

en.content:
I used to think saving money was for a house.
Then I thought it was for retirement.
Then I thought it was for family.

Last month I opened my banking app
and realized I'd misunderstood.

Saving money is for one thing:
making sure the autopay doesn't fail.

Rent, insurance, subscriptions, taxes, utilities, credit cards —
they show up on the first of the month,
more engaged in my life than I am.

I'm just the middleman.
Money leaves the company,
spends three seconds in my account,
and continues to its real destination.

I'm not earning money.
I'm a transit station for it.

And it's an unpaid internship.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose phone lights up like a party every payday from autopay alerts — they'll laugh, then sigh, 'yeah, I'm just passing through my own account too'
variations: Bonus: I told a coworker about this. He thought for a second and said, 'You're lucky. At least the money touches your account. Mine goes straight from the company to my landlord. Saves a step. Also saves me from ever seeing the balance.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 年單格漫畫「現代存在焦慮」主題，改寫為台灣手機通知情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我手機今天彈出一則通知：
「你今年的螢幕使用時間，已經超過去年同期 18%。」

我盯著那行字看了很久。

然後我想了一個哲學問題——
如果我醒著的時間，
有 60% 在看這個小螢幕，
那我到底是活在這個世界，
還是活在這個 6.1 吋的長方形裡？

我把手機關掉，深呼吸，
抬頭看了看窗外。

外面正在下雨。
我下意識拿起手機，
想拍下來。

結果發現——
我連看雨，
都需要先讓螢幕看一遍才安心。

我不是用手機在生活，
我是手機在用我蒐集素材。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明出去玩，照片卻拍到手指抽筋、回到家還要花兩小時修圖才敢發限動的朋友——他會看完後默默把手機鎖屏，但兩分鐘後又會打開
variations: 進階版：我朋友看完這個說：「我更慘，我前幾天去看夕陽，看到一半發現我在直播，但其實沒人在看。我關掉直播後，反而有點失落，因為連夕陽都沒人陪我看。」

en.content:
My phone pinged today:
"Your screen time this year is up 18% from last year."

I stared at it for a while.

Then I had a philosophical thought —
if 60% of my waking hours
are spent looking at this tiny screen,
am I living in this world,
or inside this 6.1-inch rectangle?

I turned off the phone, took a breath,
and looked out the window.

It was raining.
I instinctively picked up my phone
to take a photo.

That's when I realized —
I can't even look at rain
without letting the screen see it first.

I'm not using my phone to live my life.
My phone is using me to gather content.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who took 200 photos on vacation and spent two more hours editing them before posting — they'll lock their screen after reading, then unlock it again two minutes later
variations: Bonus: my friend read this and said, 'I'm worse. I went to watch the sunset last week and realized halfway through I was livestreaming. Nobody was watching. When I ended the stream, I actually felt sad. Even the sunset had no audience for me.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自虛無主義迷因「面對焦慮的方法」，改寫為台灣失眠夜晚情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，我又醒了。

我睜眼看著天花板，
開始盤點我這一生欠的東西：

房貸還有 22 年。
保險還要繳到 60 歲。
爸媽的養老責任。
孩子未來的學費。
身體早晚要還的健康費。
還有跟自己約定要寫的那本書。

我數一數，
發現我這輩子需要活到 130 歲，
才能勉強把所有債都還清。

但人類平均壽命只有 80。

所以我決定不再焦慮了。

反正怎麼樣都還不完，
就讓它欠著吧。

人生不是還債，
是先享受，
再把帳單留給時間。

我翻了個身，
睡得異常香。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜失眠瘋狂滑手機、隔天還要去開會的朋友——他會看完後關燈，然後真的睡著，因為他終於想通了：反正怎樣都不夠，那就先睡再說
variations: 另一個版本：我隔天起床發現一件事——欠最多的不是錢，是睡眠。但偏偏睡眠是唯一不能借、不能分期、也不能讓別人替你還的債。所以從今天起，我把睡眠列為第一順位債主。其他人請排隊。

en.content:
Three a.m. I'm awake again.

I stare at the ceiling
and start counting everything I owe:

22 more years on the mortgage.
Insurance until I'm 60.
My parents' eventual care.
My kid's future tuition.
The health debt my body will collect.
The book I promised myself I'd write.

I add it up
and realize I'd need to live to 130
to barely break even.

The average human makes it to 80.

So I decide to stop worrying.

If I can't pay it back anyway,
I'll just let it ride.

Life isn't about clearing debt.
It's about enjoying first
and leaving the bill with time.

I roll over
and fall asleep faster than I have in years.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend doomscrolling at 3 a.m. before a morning meeting — they'll turn off the light after reading, and actually fall asleep. Because they finally accepted: nothing's ever enough, so might as well rest first
variations: Alt: I woke up the next day and realized — the biggest debt isn't money. It's sleep. And sleep is the one debt you can't borrow, can't refinance, and can't have anyone else pay off for you. So from today, sleep is my first-priority creditor. Everyone else, please form a line.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自自嘲笑話「健身與意志力」主題，改寫為台灣 7-11 健身房對比情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我新年立志要每週健身三次。

我辦了健身房年費，
買了瑜珈墊、阻力帶、運動鞋。
還下載了三個健身 App。

四個月過去了。

我去過健身房一次——
是註冊那天，行政流程必須到場。

但我發現一件事：
我每週會走進 7-11 大概 12 次。

所以我修正了我的目標：

「每週健身三次」改成
「每週去 7-11 三次」。

達成率瞬間從 0% 變 400%。

人生的智慧不是改變自己，
而是修改 KPI。

我立刻拍照發限動，
標題寫：
「自律的男人最帥。」

沒有人需要知道內容是什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個年初發誓要減肥、現在已經五月還在原地踏步的朋友——他會大笑然後立刻去 7-11 買關東煮，並把這當成今天的健身打卡
variations: 進階版：我同事看完說：「我比你更厲害，我把健身房年費當成捐款，每年定期支持一個我永遠不會去的場所，這叫做支持地方產業。」

en.content:
New Year's resolution: hit the gym three times a week.

I bought the annual membership.
A yoga mat. Resistance bands. New shoes.
Downloaded three fitness apps.

Four months later.

I've been to the gym exactly once —
on sign-up day, when paperwork required my presence.

But I noticed something:
I walk into 7-Eleven about 12 times a week.

So I revised my goal:

"Gym three times a week" became
"7-Eleven three times a week."

My completion rate jumped from 0% to 400%.

The secret to life isn't changing yourself.
It's editing the KPIs.

I immediately posted a story:
"Discipline looks good on a man."

Nobody needs to know what the discipline was for.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who swore they'd lose weight in January and hasn't moved by May — they'll laugh, then walk straight to 7-Eleven for snacks and count it as today's gym session
variations: Bonus: my coworker read this and said, 'I'm one level above you. I treat my gym membership as a donation — annual recurring support for a place I will never enter. I call it supporting local business.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自「成年人累的本質」迷因主題，改寫為台灣捷運通勤睡著情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我今天搭捷運回家，
站著睡著了。

旁邊的阿姨輕輕拍我肩膀：
「弟弟，你站到了。」

我看了一下站名——
我搭過頭了七站。

阿姨笑著說：
「沒關係啦，年輕人累。」

我反過來問她：
「阿姨，你怎麼沒睡？」

她想了一下：
「我已經沒有需要趕著回去的地方了，
所以我可以慢慢看風景。」

「你呢？你急著回去做什麼？」

我愣了一下。

回去煮晚餐、洗衣服、
滑半小時手機、
然後設定明早六點半的鬧鐘。

沒有一件事是不能再等的。

但我還是急。

阿姨拍了拍我，下車前說：
「你有事可以急，是好事。
等到沒事可急的那天，
你會想念現在這樣站著睡著的自己。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個下班搭車就秒睡、卻說自己沒有失眠問題的朋友——他會看完後若有所思，然後傳一句「靠，我搞不好沒被阿姨叫醒過」
variations: 後來我下車後想：阿姨那句話比任何老闆說的「加油」都更讓我想哭。因為老闆要我急，是怕我慢了；阿姨叫我珍惜急，是因為她知道——人有一天會什麼都不急了，那一天不是解脫，是孤單。

en.content:
I rode the metro home today
and fell asleep standing up.

The woman next to me tapped my shoulder:
"Hey kid, this is your stop."

I checked the station —
I'd missed mine by seven stops.

She smiled.
"It's fine. Young people are tired."

I asked her,
"How come you didn't fall asleep?"

She thought about it.
"I don't have anywhere I need to rush back to anymore.
So I take my time and watch the view."

"What about you? What are you rushing home for?"

I paused.

Make dinner. Do laundry.
Scroll for thirty minutes.
Set a 6:30 alarm for tomorrow.

None of it couldn't have waited.

But I was still rushing.

She patted my shoulder before stepping off:
"Having something to rush for is a good thing.
The day you don't,
you'll miss the version of yourself
who fell asleep standing up."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who passes out the second they sit on the train but insists they 'don't have insomnia' — they'll go quiet, then text back, 'damn, what if no auntie has ever woken me up'
variations: Later I thought about it: what she said hit harder than any boss telling me 'keep at it.' My boss wants me to rush because he's worried I'm slow. The auntie wanted me to cherish the rush because she knew — one day there'll be nothing left to rush for. That day isn't relief. It's loneliness.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026「AI-Doomerism」職場迷因主題，改寫為台灣中年工程師被 AI 取代的場景，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
人資部約我談話。

「公司導入了新的 AI 系統，
它可以做你 90% 的工作。」

我點點頭。

「但我們還是想留下你，
因為剩下那 10%，
是 AI 學不會的。」

我感動到差點哭出來：
「真的嗎？是哪 10%？」

人資翻了翻資料：
「就是負責對它說『辛苦了』、
幫它泡咖啡、
然後在它出錯的時候，
用人類的身分去跟客戶道歉。」

我懂了。

從今天開始，
我的職位正式從「資深工程師」
改為「AI 的情緒勞動者」。

薪水沒變，
但工作內容終於對得起這個薪水了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個常常加班、然後說「以後 AI 來做就好」的工程師朋友——他會邊苦笑邊轉發，因為他知道這個劇本遲早會輪到他演
variations: 進階版：我朋友問我，那 AI 出錯多嗎？我說：「不多，但每次出錯都會被歸咎於人類沒有好好引導它——所以問題其實永遠都是我，AI 只是一個沒有犯錯能力的同事。」

en.content:
HR called me in for a chat.

"The company's rolled out a new AI system.
It can do about 90% of your job."

I nodded.

"But we want to keep you,
because the other 10%
is stuff the AI can't learn."

I almost teared up.
"Really? What's the 10%?"

HR flipped through some pages.
"Saying 'good job' to it.
Making its coffee.
And, when it screws up,
apologizing to the client as a human."

I got it.

Starting today,
my title officially changes
from 'Senior Engineer'
to 'Emotional Labor for the AI.'

Same salary.
But now it finally matches the work.
en.editorNote: Send to the engineer friend who always works late and says 'AI will handle this someday' — they'll laugh bitterly while forwarding it, because they know the script is coming for them
variations: Bonus: my friend asked, does the AI mess up a lot? I said, 'Not really. But every time it does, the blame goes to a human for not guiding it correctly. So the problem is always me. The AI is just a coworker incapable of being wrong.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 一句式黑色幽默「I'm fine, terms and conditions apply」，改寫為台灣 LINE 對話情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友傳 LINE 問我：「最近還好嗎？」

我打了「還好」兩個字，
然後盯著螢幕看了五分鐘。

後來我把「還好」改成：

「還好 *」

*條款與細則：
本人聲明之「還好」適用於：
白天上班時段、面對長輩、
以及尚未打開銀行 App 的時刻。

不適用於：
凌晨三點、領完薪水第三天、
以及任何被問到「五年後想做什麼」的對話。

本聲明可隨時撤回，
撤回時不另行通知。

朋友回我：「了解，我也是。」

我們連笑話都不用解釋，
就懂了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你每次問他「最近怎樣」、他都秒回「還好」的朋友——他會看完笑出來，然後跟你說「靠，被你抓到了」
variations: 進階版：後來我發現「還好」是台灣人最高深的密碼學——同一個詞，可以代表「我快崩潰了但不想講」、也可以代表「真的還好」、還可以代表「不要再問了拜託」。怎麼解讀全憑收訊者的修為。

en.content:
A friend texted: "How are you doing lately?"

I typed "I'm fine"
and stared at the screen for five minutes.

Then I changed it to:

"I'm fine *"

*Terms and conditions:
My declaration of 'fine' applies during:
daytime work hours, conversations with elders,
and any moment before I open my banking app.

Does not apply during:
3 a.m., the third day after payday,
or any conversation involving the phrase
'where do you see yourself in five years.'

This statement may be withdrawn at any time
without further notice.

My friend replied: "Got it. Same."

We didn't even have to explain the joke.
We both knew.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who replies 'fine' within two seconds every time you ask how they're doing — they'll laugh and text back, 'damn, you caught me'
variations: Bonus: 'fine' is the most advanced cryptography in modern friendship. Same word can mean 'I'm spiraling but won't say it,' or 'genuinely fine,' or 'please stop asking.' Decoding depends entirely on the receiver's spiritual training.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 黑色幽默「我靠管理期望值來活著」主題，改寫為台灣相親對話情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
相親對象問我：
「你對人生的目標是什麼？」

我認真想了一下，回答：

「我目前的人生策略是：
把所有期望值降到最低，
這樣只要還活著、
還能呼吸、
還能在禮拜五下班前把信箱清完，
就算是賺到。」

她聽完愣了很久，
然後說：

「我覺得我們很合。
我也是這樣活的。」

我們交換了 LINE，
約好下次一起去 IKEA。
不是因為要買家具——
是因為那裡的試躺床位很多，
而我們都很累。

後來我跟朋友說，
那一刻我知道這個女生有戲：

會把降低期望值當成人生策略的人，
通常都很善良。
因為她已經放棄了
要這個世界對她好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直被催婚、卻覺得相親很辛苦的朋友——他看完會笑，然後突然覺得也許下一場相親可以試著聊「期望值管理」，反正最差也就這樣
variations: 另一個版本：第二次見面她跟我說：「我喜歡你的原因，是你不會讓我覺得我必須變成另一個人。因為你自己也不打算變成另一個人。」——這大概是我聽過最浪漫的一句話：兩個放棄掙扎的人，決定一起放棄。

en.content:
On a blind date, she asked:
"What's your goal in life?"

I thought about it seriously and said:

"My current life strategy
is to lower all expectations to the floor,
so as long as I'm still alive,
still breathing,
and still able to clear my inbox before Friday EOD,
I count the day as a win."

She stared at me for a while.
Then said:

"I think we're compatible.
I live the same way."

We traded numbers
and agreed to go to IKEA next time.
Not to buy furniture —
the display beds are great for napping,
and we were both tired.

Later I told a friend:
that moment I knew she was a keeper.

People who treat 'lower your expectations'
as a life strategy
are usually kind.
Because they've already given up
on the world being kind to them.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's tired of being pressured into dating — they'll laugh, then maybe try opening with 'expectation management' on their next date, since worst case it stays the same
variations: Alt: At our second meeting she said, 'What I like about you is you don't make me feel like I have to become someone else. Because you've also given up on becoming someone else.' That's the most romantic line I've ever heard. Two people who've stopped struggling, deciding to stop together.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026「黑色幽默是現代生存技能」主題文章，改寫為台灣健檢報告情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我去做健康檢查。

醫生看著報告，眉頭皺起來：
「你這個身體狀況，
再這樣下去，
大概還能撐 30 年。」

我馬上抬頭問：
「保證 30 年？」

醫生愣了一下：
「不是保證，是『最多』。」

我說：
「沒關係，30 年很夠了。
我的房貸還剩 22 年，
還有 8 年可以好好享受人生。」

醫生放下筆，認真看著我：
「先生，我建議你開始運動。」

我說：
「為什麼？這樣我可以多活幾年嗎？」

醫生：
「不是。是這樣你還貸款的時候，
比較不會喘不過氣。」

我們對視了三秒，
然後一起笑出來。

那是我這個月，
第一次發自內心地笑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛買房、然後開始懷疑自己是不是用未來換現在的朋友——他會看完先嘆氣，然後說「我也想要一個會講笑話的醫生」
variations: 進階版：後來我問醫生：「那如果我運動了，可以還清貸款後再多活幾年嗎？」醫生說：「可以，但你要選擇一個——多活的那幾年用來休息，還是用來繼續工作賺錢，給下一代買房？」我沒回答，因為我們都知道答案。

en.content:
I went in for a health checkup.

The doctor frowned at the report.
"Given your condition,
if you keep going like this,
you've got maybe 30 more years."

I looked up immediately:
"Guaranteed 30?"

The doctor blinked.
"Not guaranteed. Maximum."

I said:
"That's fine. 30 is plenty.
My mortgage runs 22 more years,
so I get 8 to actually enjoy life."

The doctor put down his pen
and looked at me seriously:
"Sir, I'd recommend you start exercising."

I asked:
"Why? So I live longer?"

Doctor:
"No. So you don't run out of breath
while making mortgage payments."

We held eye contact for three seconds,
then burst out laughing together.

That was the first time this month
I'd laughed from the gut.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just bought a house and started suspecting they traded their future for the present — they'll sigh first, then say 'I want a doctor who tells jokes too'
variations: Bonus: I asked the doctor, 'If I exercise, can I have a few extra years after the loan's paid off?' He said, 'Sure. But pick one — those extra years for rest, or for keeping working so the next generation can buy a house?' I didn't answer. We both knew.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 Joe Lennon 單格漫畫「科技背叛你」主題，改寫為台灣手機鬧鐘情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我手機的鬧鐘有十三個。

第一個叫「真的該起床了」。
第二個叫「不起來會遲到」。
第三個叫「現在起來還來得及」。
第七個叫「你已經遲到了」。
第十一個叫「你今天死定了」。
第十三個叫「沒事，反正你已經放棄了」。

每一個我都按了「貪睡」。

後來我意識到——
我不是在跟鬧鐘戰鬥，
我是在跟一個更早起床、
更有野心、
更相信明天會更好的自己
談判。

而那個版本的我，
每一天都輸給現在的我。

我有點對不起他。
但又覺得，
他自己應該也累了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天都要設五個以上鬧鐘的朋友——他會邊看邊笑邊點頭，然後問你「為什麼你會知道我手機裡的鬧鐘都長這樣」
variations: 進階版：最近我把第十三個鬧鐘的名字改成「歡迎回到地球」——因為我發現，與其逼自己當另一個人，不如先承認自己今天已經盡力了。連起床都是一種勇敢。

en.content:
I have thirteen alarms on my phone.

The first one is named "Time to actually get up."
The second one is "You'll be late."
The third one is "You can still make it."
The seventh one is "You're already late."
The eleventh one is "You're cooked today."
The thirteenth one is "It's fine, you've already given up."

I hit snooze on every single one.

Later I realized —
I wasn't fighting the alarms.
I was negotiating
with an earlier, more ambitious version of myself
who still believed tomorrow would be better.

And that version of me
loses to the current me
every single day.

I feel a little bad for him.
But honestly,
he's probably tired too.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sets five or more alarms every morning — they'll laugh while nodding, then ask 'how did you know my alarms are named exactly like this'
variations: Bonus: I renamed alarm thirteen to 'welcome back to Earth.' Instead of forcing myself to be a different person, I just admit I did my best today. Even getting out of bed counts as courage.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 職場黑色幽默「meeting that could have been an email」主題，改寫為台灣會議室情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
公司開了一個一小時的會議，
主題是「如何提升工作效率」。

會議開到第四十分鐘，
主管說：
「我們時間有點不夠了，
下週再開一次延伸會議。」

沒有人反對。
因為大家都知道——
反對的人，
會被指派去寫會議紀錄。

會議結束後，
我打開待辦清單，
發現上面寫著：
「整理本週開過的會議，
做一份效率分析簡報。」

那是我這禮拜的
第三個類似任務。

我突然懂了：
我們不是在工作，
我們是在開「關於工作的會議」。
而真正的工作，
是夾在會議空檔，
用三十分鐘
硬塞完八小時的份量。

但沒關係。
至少薪水是發八小時的。
雖然心力是耗了十六小時的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天行事曆上排滿會議、卻一直抱怨「沒時間做事」的同事——他看完會嘆氣，然後說「我下午剛好有一個討論這個的會」
variations: 進階版：後來我提了一個建議：「不如把所有會議改成 email？」主管說：「好主意。我們下週開個會討論一下這個方案。」我沉默了兩秒，然後笑了。在這種地方笑，是唯一的逃生出口。

en.content:
The company held a one-hour meeting
on "how to improve work efficiency."

Forty minutes in, my manager said:
"We're a bit short on time.
Let's schedule a follow-up next week."

Nobody objected.
Because we all knew —
whoever objects
gets assigned to take the minutes.

After the meeting,
I opened my to-do list
and found a new task:
"Compile this week's meetings
into an efficiency analysis deck."

That was the third
similar task this week.

Then it hit me:
We're not working.
We're holding meetings about work.
The actual work
gets crammed into the gaps,
thirty minutes squeezing in
eight hours of output.

But whatever.
At least the paycheck is for eight hours.
Even if the burnout is for sixteen.
en.editorNote: Send to the colleague whose calendar is wall-to-wall meetings while they keep complaining 'I have no time to actually do anything' — they'll sigh, then say 'I have a meeting about this in the afternoon'
variations: Bonus: I once suggested, 'What if we replaced all meetings with emails?' My manager said, 'Great idea. Let's schedule a meeting next week to discuss that.' I went quiet for two seconds, then laughed. In a place like this, laughing is the only fire exit.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 虛無主義迷因「植物比我活得好」主題，改寫為台灣陽台多肉情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我陽台養了一盆多肉。

半年沒澆水，
它還活得好好的。
葉片飽滿，
光澤動人，
看起來比我有元氣。

我每天喝兩公升的水、
吃三餐、
睡七小時、
吃綜合維他命，
結果照鏡子的時候，
是它在發光，不是我。

後來我跟朋友說：
「我覺得這盆多肉的人生，
比我成功。」

朋友想了想，回我：
「對啊。
因為它沒有 KPI、
沒有貸款、
沒有人問它今年的目標。
它只要活著就好。
而你被要求活得很好。」

我看著陽台那盆多肉，
第一次羨慕一個植物。

但又想——
至少我會羨慕、會反省、會難過。
它可能連這些都沒有。

那我們到底誰比較幸運？
我也不知道。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天累得像被榨乾、卻還是要回訊息「最近不錯啦」的朋友——他會看完安靜很久，然後說「我家陽台也有一盆，我也輸給它」
variations: 進階版：後來我把那盆多肉送給了一個更焦慮的朋友。我跟他說：「不要照顧它，它會自己活下來。你只要看著它，學它就好。」一個月後他跟我說：「我學不會。但我至少學會了——不是每件事都需要拼命。」

en.content:
I have a succulent on my balcony.

Haven't watered it in six months.
It's still thriving.
Leaves plump,
perfectly glossy,
clearly more alive than I am.

I drink two liters of water a day,
eat three meals,
sleep seven hours,
take a multivitamin —
and when I look in the mirror,
it's glowing, not me.

I told a friend:
"I think that succulent's life
is more successful than mine."

My friend thought about it:
"Yeah.
It has no KPIs,
no mortgage,
no one asking about its goals this year.
It just has to be alive.
You're required to be alive *well*."

I looked at the plant.
For the first time,
I envied a piece of vegetation.

But then —
at least I can envy, reflect, feel sad.
It probably can't do any of that.

So who's actually luckier?
I honestly don't know.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's running on fumes but still texts back 'doing okay!' — they'll go quiet, then reply 'I have one on my balcony too, I also lost to it'
variations: Bonus: I gave the succulent to a more anxious friend. I told him: 'Don't take care of it. It'll survive on its own. Just watch it. Learn from it.' A month later he said, 'I couldn't learn. But I did learn one thing — not everything needs to be fought for.'

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 工作壓力黑色幽默「我把所有夢想存在 Google Drive 裡」主題，改寫為台灣資料夾整理情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我整理 Google Drive，
發現一個資料夾叫「未來計畫」。

打開來看——

2018 年：學吉他。
2019 年：去冰島。
2020 年：學寫小說。
2021 年：辭職環島。
2022 年：開咖啡店。
2023 年：考潛水證照。
2024 年：學西班牙文。
2025 年：年底前換跑道。
2026 年：（空白）

那個空白讓我盯了很久。

不是因為今年沒夢想，
而是因為我終於懂了——

前面那些不是「計畫」，
是我每年用來告訴自己
「我還有未來」的證據。

證據壞掉了，沒關係。
至少證明過我曾經想過。

後來我把那個資料夾改名，
從「未來計畫」
改成「曾經想過的事」。

沒有比較難過，
反而比較誠實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每年新年都立志、然後一年後默默刪除的朋友——他會看完笑著說「靠，我也有一模一樣的資料夾」，然後沉默
variations: 進階版：後來我在資料夾底下新增了一個檔案，名字叫「今天有好好吃飯」。我發現比起一年後的計畫，今天的飯更實在。夢想會背叛你，但晚餐不會。

en.content:
I was cleaning out Google Drive
and found a folder called "Future Plans."

I opened it —

2018: Learn guitar.
2019: Trip to Iceland.
2020: Write a novel.
2021: Quit job, travel the island.
2022: Open a café.
2023: Get diving certified.
2024: Learn Spanish.
2025: Switch careers by year-end.
2026: (blank)

I stared at the blank for a long time.

Not because I had no dreams this year.
Because I finally understood —

Those weren't "plans."
They were yearly evidence
I gave myself
that I still had a future.

The evidence didn't work out. That's fine.
At least it proved I once imagined.

Later I renamed the folder.
From "Future Plans"
to "Things I Once Considered."

It didn't make me sadder.
Just more honest.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sets New Year's goals every January and quietly deletes them by December — they'll laugh and say 'damn, I have the exact same folder,' then go quiet
variations: Bonus: I added a new file at the bottom of the folder called 'Ate well today.' I realized today's dinner is more real than next year's plan. Dreams will betray you. Dinner won't.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 黑色幽默「我把焦慮命名為 Jeff」主題，改寫為台灣便利商店情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我把我的焦慮取名叫做小明。

這樣每次它出現的時候，
我就可以說：
「啊，又是小明來了。」

聽起來像是
國中同學突然來找你，
而不是
一個從胸口蔓延到指尖、
讓你在便利商店
站著看茶葉蛋
站了七分鐘
忘記自己原本要買什麼
的那種感覺。

後來我跟朋友說：
「其實小明還滿準時的。
每天晚上十一點半
一定會出現，
比我認識的所有人都還守信用。」

朋友想了想，回我：
「那你應該感謝它。
至少這個世界上
還有一個東西
沒有放你鴿子。」

我笑了一下。
然後又站著看了五分鐘茶葉蛋。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直說「我還好」、但你知道他半夜都會傳一些奇怪訊息的朋友——他會看完笑著說「我家小明叫做阿志，每天十二點準時報到」
variations: 進階版：後來我把小明加進通訊錄，備註寫「老朋友」。這樣每次它在通知列冒出來，我就不用嚇一跳。承認它存在，比假裝沒看見還省力。

en.content:
I named my anxiety Kevin.

That way, every time it shows up,
I can just say:
"Oh, Kevin again."

It sounds like
an old classmate dropping by,
rather than
a thing that creeps from your chest
to your fingertips
and makes you stand in front of the
convenience store sandwich shelf
for seven minutes
forgetting what you came in for.

Later I told a friend:
"Honestly, Kevin's pretty reliable.
Shows up every night at 11:30 sharp,
more punctual than anyone I know."

My friend thought for a second:
"Then you should thank him.
At least one thing in this world
hasn't ghosted you."

I laughed.
Then stood at the sandwich shelf
for another five minutes.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'I'm okay' but sends weird texts at 2 a.m. — they'll laugh and say 'mine's named Greg, shows up at midnight on the dot'
variations: Bonus: I added Kevin to my contacts, labeled 'old friend.' That way when his name pops up in notifications, I don't flinch. Admitting something exists costs less energy than pretending it doesn't.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 存在主義迷因「同一個煩惱、更新版圖形」主題，改寫為台灣手機系統升級情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
今天手機跳出更新通知：

「最新版本已準備好。
本次更新包含：
效能改善、安全性強化、
以及多項您不會注意到的優化。」

我看完愣住。

那一行字描述的
根本不是我的手機，
是我的人生。

每年年底總結，
我也都這樣寫：

「過去一年，
我做了一些自我成長。
包含：心態微調、
抗壓性升級、
以及多項我自己也不確定有沒有真的改變的部分。」

按下「立即更新」。
手機重啟之後，
看起來和昨天一模一樣。

我也是。

但工程師說
背景的程式有變強。
我也選擇相信
自己背景的程式有變強。

畢竟人總要相信點什麼，
才能繼續用。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個年底總是會發「今年我學到了好多」貼文、但其實也說不出學到什麼的朋友——他會邊笑邊轉發，然後在底下留言「這個年度回顧範本我可以直接套用」
variations: 進階版：後來我發現，人生跟手機系統一樣——你以為大改版會讓一切變好，但其實真正讓人活下去的，是那些每天默默運作、永遠不會被寫進更新說明的小程式。比如：今天還願意起床。

en.content:
My phone pushed an update notification today:

"A new version is ready.
This update includes:
performance improvements, security enhancements,
and various optimizations you won't notice."

I stared at it.

That line wasn't describing my phone.
It was describing my life.

Every year-end recap I write
looks exactly the same:

"Over the past year,
I've grown a little.
Including: mindset adjustments,
improved stress tolerance,
and other things I'm not actually sure changed."

I tapped "Update now."
The phone rebooted
and looked identical to yesterday.

So did I.

But the engineers say
the background processes are stronger now.
I choose to believe
my background processes are stronger too.

You have to believe something
to keep using yourself.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who posts 'I learned so much this year' every December but can't actually name what they learned — they'll laugh, share it, and comment 'I'm just copy-pasting this as my year-end recap'
variations: Bonus: I realized life is like a phone OS — you think the big release will fix everything, but the things that actually keep you running are the tiny background processes nobody writes in the patch notes. Like: still being willing to get out of bed today.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 千禧世代成人困境「過度思考是我唯一的有氧」主題，改寫為台灣半夜睡前情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
醫生問我：
「你平常有運動嗎？」

我認真想了一下：
「有。
每天晚上一點到三點，
我會躺在床上
進行高強度的『回想三年前
說錯一句話』訓練。」

醫生抬起頭。

我繼續說：
「附加項目還有
『重新編排當時應該怎麼回』、
『假設那個人現在還記不記得』、
以及『推測對方有沒有偷偷討厭我』。」

醫生放下筆：
「先生，那叫做焦慮，不叫運動。」

我說：
「但是隔天我真的有出汗，
而且心跳很快。」

醫生想了三秒，
然後嘆了一口氣：
「好吧，至少有運動到。」

我們對視了一下。

那是這個月第一次
有人沒有反駁我，
讓我莫名覺得被理解。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜會傳「你還記得三年前那次嗎？」訊息給你的朋友——他看完會說「靠，這就是我」，然後馬上又開始想三年前的事
variations: 進階版：後來我發現，半夜的腦袋是一台壞掉的時光機，會把你最不想去的那一天當成首頁。但既然壞了，與其修，不如改成觀光行程：去那一天看看，然後告訴當時的自己，現在的你還活著。這樣也算有療效。

en.content:
The doctor asked:
"Do you exercise regularly?"

I thought about it:
"Yes.
Every night between 1 and 3 a.m.,
I lie in bed
doing high-intensity
'remembering something I said three years ago' training."

The doctor looked up.

I kept going:
"Supplementary drills include
'rewriting what I should have said,'
'guessing if the other person still remembers,'
and 'estimating whether they secretly hate me.'"

The doctor put down his pen:
"Sir, that's anxiety, not exercise."

I said:
"But I actually sweat the next morning.
And my heart rate's elevated."

The doctor paused for three seconds,
then sighed:
"Okay. Technically that counts as a workout."

We held eye contact.

That was the first time this month
someone didn't argue with me.
I inexplicably felt seen.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who texts you at 2 a.m. 'do you remember that time three years ago?' — they'll say 'this is literally me,' then immediately start thinking about that time three years ago
variations: Bonus: I realized the late-night brain is a broken time machine — it always boots up on the day you least want to revisit. But since it's broken anyway, stop trying to fix it. Treat it like a tourist trip: visit that day, tell your past self that present-you is still alive. That counts as therapy.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 過勞職場黑色幽默「我已經把自己外包給自己」主題，改寫為台灣下班接案情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我下班後接了一個案子。

甲方在群組裡催進度。
我打開電腦，
開始加班。

寫到一半我才意識到——
這個甲方就是我自己。

白天的我接了案，
說「沒問題、晚上做得完」。

晚上的我打開檔案，
看到時程表，
忍不住小聲罵了一句：
「白天那個傢伙
到底在想什麼？」

後來我跟朋友說：
「我已經把自己外包給自己了。
白天接案，晚上做工，
薪水各扣一輪手續費，
剩下的拿去買咖啡，
讓明天的自己
繼續接案給後天的自己。」

朋友問：
「那你什麼時候休息？」

我說：
「等這個閉環崩潰的那一天。」

朋友沉默了三秒，
然後說：
「那你要不要先預約一下，
免得到時候大家都在崩潰，
排不到位置。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個下班還在回 LINE、週末還在改稿、但永遠覺得「快忙完了」的朋友——他會看完苦笑，然後馬上又打開電腦繼續做，因為這個笑話本身就是他
variations: 進階版：後來我發現，這個閉環其實沒有崩潰的一天。會崩潰的不是系統，是裡面那個「自以為還撐得住」的人。所以與其等系統崩潰，不如自己選一天，溫柔地把自己從這個循環裡拔出來。哪怕只是請半天假，去公園坐著什麼都不做。

en.content:
I took on a side gig after work.

The client kept pinging the group chat
for updates.
I opened my laptop
and started overtime.

Halfway through, it hit me —
the client was me.

Daytime-me took the job,
promising "no problem, I'll finish it tonight."

Nighttime-me opened the file,
saw the timeline,
and muttered:
"What was that guy this morning thinking?"

Later I told a friend:
"I've outsourced myself to myself.
Daytime takes the contract,
nighttime does the labor,
each side skims a service fee,
and the rest goes to coffee
so tomorrow-me
can take a job from day-after-tomorrow-me."

My friend asked:
"When do you rest?"

I said:
"The day this loop collapses."

My friend went quiet for three seconds:
"You might want to book a slot now.
When everyone's loop collapses at once,
the waiting list will be brutal."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who replies to Slack after hours and edits drafts on weekends but always says 'almost done' — they'll smile bitterly, then immediately reopen their laptop, because this joke is just them
variations: Bonus: I figured out the loop never actually collapses on its own. What collapses is the person inside who keeps insisting they can still handle it. So don't wait for the system to break — pick a day, gently lift yourself out. Even if it's just a half-day off, sitting in a park doing nothing.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 黑色幽默迷因「天空顏色怪怪的還是要上班」主題，改寫為台灣通勤情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
早上七點半，
我打開窗戶準備出門上班。

天空是橘紅色的。
不是日出那種橘紅，
是有點像 PS 濾鏡開錯的那種橘紅。

新聞跑馬燈寫：
「沙塵暴混合霾害，
建議民眾減少外出。」

我看了一眼，
然後繼續綁鞋帶。

捷運上人擠人，
大家都戴著口罩，
沒有人在討論天空。

到了公司，
主管說：
「今天有點累對不對？
是不是熬夜了？」

我說：
「沒有，是天空的顏色怪怪的。」

主管笑了：
「哎呀，那是濾鏡啦。
你想太多。」

我看著螢幕上的待辦清單，
突然想：
如果有一天
天空裂開、海水倒灌、
地殼移動，
我大概也會準時九點打卡，
然後在群組裡說：
「我會晚十分鐘，捷運停了。」

世界末日來的時候，
我可能還在處理上週的會議紀錄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個颱風天還在工作、地震完五分鐘就回去打字的朋友——他會苦笑一下，然後說「我昨天聽到防空警報的第一反應是先存檔」
variations: 進階版：後來我才發現，所謂的「正常生活」其實是一種集體催眠。大家都假裝沒看到天空，是因為一旦承認天空怪怪的，就要承認很多事情其實也都怪怪的。然後就會發現——還能繼續上班，已經是一種奇蹟。

en.content:
7:30 a.m.
I opened the window to head to work.

The sky was orange-red.
Not sunrise orange-red.
More like someone left a Photoshop filter on by mistake.

The news ticker said:
"Sandstorm and smog warning.
Residents advised to stay indoors."

I glanced at it,
then kept tying my shoes.

The subway was packed.
Everyone wore masks.
Nobody talked about the sky.

At the office, my manager said:
"You look tired today.
Did you stay up late?"

I said:
"No, the sky's a weird color."

He laughed:
"That's just a filter. You're overthinking."

I looked at my to-do list
and suddenly thought:
if one day
the sky cracked open,
the ocean flipped over,
the tectonic plates shifted —
I would still clock in at nine,
then post in the group chat:
"Running ten minutes late, subway's down."

When the world ends,
I'll probably still be cleaning up
last week's meeting notes.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who works through typhoons and is back at the keyboard five minutes after an earthquake — they'll laugh and say 'my first reflex when I heard the air raid siren yesterday was to hit save'
variations: Bonus: I realized 'normal life' is a kind of collective hypnosis. Everyone pretends the sky looks fine because admitting it doesn't would mean admitting a lot of other things don't either. And then you'd notice — just being able to keep going to work is already a small miracle.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 成人黑色幽默「我的興趣是付帳單」主題，改寫為台灣三十歲生活情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
公司舉辦員工自我介紹活動，
表格上有一欄寫著「個人興趣」。

我盯著那一欄想了二十分鐘，
最後填了：
「準時繳房租。」

主管看到，笑了：
「這算興趣嗎？」

我認真回他：
「算啊。
而且我為它投入了大量心力，
每個月固定練習一次，
從未中斷，
還持續精進——
從以前的『差點來不及』
進化到現在的『提前一天就匯』。」

主管笑不出來了。

他低頭看了一下
自己的表格。

「興趣」那一欄
也是空白的。

他抓著筆想了三秒，
寫了：
「準時繳貸款。」

那天下午我們在茶水間遇到，
他遞給我一杯黑咖啡，
說：
「歡迎加入。」

我說：
「謝謝，但其實我已經是會員很久了。
只是這是我第一次
在履歷上承認。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個三十歲之後越來越不知道「興趣」要填什麼的朋友——他會看完哈哈大笑，然後說「我已經很多年沒有除了賺錢之外的興趣了，但我不敢說出來」
variations: 進階版：後來我把那張表格貼在冰箱上。每次想抱怨人生很無聊的時候，就看一眼，提醒自己——能準時繳房租、準時還貸款、準時餵飽自己的人，已經贏過很多正在掙扎的人。無聊有時候是一種特權。

en.content:
The company hosted a team introduction event
with a form that asked for "personal hobbies."

I stared at that field for twenty minutes
and finally wrote:
"Paying rent on time."

My manager saw it and laughed:
"Does that count as a hobby?"

I told him, completely seriously:
"It does.
I've invested a lot of effort into it,
practice once a month without fail,
never missed a session,
and keep improving —
from the old days of 'barely on time'
to today's 'transferring a day early.'"

He stopped laughing.

He looked down
at his own form.

The "hobbies" line
was blank.

He held his pen for three seconds,
then wrote:
"Paying the mortgage on time."

That afternoon we ran into each other
in the break room.
He handed me a black coffee:
"Welcome."

I said:
"Thanks. I've actually been a member for a while.
This is just the first time
I've put it on paper."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can never figure out what to put in 'hobbies' after turning thirty — they'll laugh and say 'I haven't had a hobby besides making money in years, I just never admitted it out loud'
variations: Bonus: I stuck the form on my fridge. Whenever I want to complain that life is boring, I look at it and remind myself — anyone who pays rent on time, pays the loan on time, feeds themselves on time, has already beaten a lot of people who are still drowning. Boring is sometimes a privilege.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 單格漫畫黑色幽默「科技背叛你」主題，改寫為台灣手機客服情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我的手機螢幕摔裂了。

打電話到客服。
語音說：
「請選擇您的服務項目，
螢幕維修請按一。」

我按一。

語音說：
「請拍下您手機螢幕的照片，
上傳到我們的線上系統。」

我看著手中
螢幕已經裂成蜘蛛網的手機，
沉默了很久。

打回客服。
問語音：
「我可以講真人嗎？」

語音說：
「為了提供更快速的服務，
請先在 App 內申請。」

我打開 App，
App 跳出更新通知：
「更新後才能使用此功能。」

按下更新，
系統提示：
「請確認 Wi-Fi 連線穩定，
避免螢幕中斷。」

螢幕中斷。

我把手機放下，
看著它裂掉的反光裡
那個有點疲倦的自己，
突然想——

科技不是壞了。
它只是進化成不需要我們的版本了。

而我們還在這裡，
按一、按二、按三，
試著證明自己
還沒有被淘汰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次打客服都會被語音轉接十幾次的朋友——他會看完無奈大笑，然後說「上次我為了處理一個帳單問題，花了三個小時跟機器人對話」
variations: 進階版：後來我跑去實體門市。店員看了一眼手機，說：「先生，這個沒辦法現場修，要送回原廠。」我問：「那我要怎麼跟你們聯絡？」她遞給我一張紙：「請掃這個 QR Code。」我看著手中那台裂掉到看不清楚 QR Code 的手機，第一次在二十一世紀感受到一種純粹的、原始的、無能為力的安靜。

en.content:
My phone screen shattered.

I called customer service.
The robot said:
"Please select your issue.
For screen repair, press one."

I pressed one.

"Please take a photo of your screen
and upload it through our online system."

I looked at the phone in my hand,
screen cracked into a spiderweb,
and stayed quiet for a long time.

I called back.
"Can I speak to a human?"

"For faster service,
please submit a request through our app."

I opened the app.
The app demanded an update:
"Update required to access this feature."

I tapped update.
A prompt appeared:
"Please ensure stable Wi-Fi
to avoid interruption."

The screen went dark.

I set the phone down
and caught my own slightly exhausted reflection
in the cracked glass.

Suddenly I thought —

Technology isn't broken.
It just evolved into a version
that doesn't need us anymore.

And we're still here,
pressing one, two, three,
trying to prove
we haven't been deprecated.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been transferred through fifteen robot menus on every support call — they'll laugh helplessly and say 'last time it took me three hours of arguing with a bot to fix one billing issue'
variations: Bonus: I went to the physical store. The clerk glanced at the phone: 'Sir, we can't fix this here. It has to go back to the manufacturer.' I asked, 'How do I follow up?' She handed me a slip of paper: 'Scan this QR code.' Holding a phone too shattered to read the QR code, I experienced a pure, primal, twenty-first-century silence of total helplessness.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 黑色幽默存活技能「我學會了在一切都不太好的時候笑」主題，改寫為台灣健保看診情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我去看醫生。

醫生看著健檢報告，
眉頭微皺：
「你睡眠不足、
肝指數偏高、
血壓有點高、
膽固醇也超標。」

我點點頭。

醫生問：
「你工作壓力大嗎？」

我說：
「還好。」

醫生說：
「你每天工作幾小時？」

我說：
「十二到十四個小時。」

醫生看了我一眼。

我補充：
「但我覺得我心態還滿好的，
常常會自己跟自己開玩笑。」

醫生說：
「能舉個例子嗎？」

我認真想了一下：
「比方說早上看到自己的黑眼圈，
我會笑著說
『今天看起來特別有靈魂』。
或是加班到半夜十二點，
會跟自己說
『今天的月亮真美
是給認真工作的人看的』。」

醫生沉默了三秒，
然後在病歷上寫了什麼。

我偷瞄了一眼。

上面寫著：
「患者具備高度黑色幽默能力，
可能是長期壓力下的代償機制，
建議——
減少工作時數，
增加真正的快樂。」

我看完想笑，
但又不太確定
是不是應該笑。

那大概就是
我目前最大的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是用「沒事啦」帶過所有事情、但你知道他其實累到不行的朋友——他會看完很久，然後傳一個哭笑表情，再傳一句「靠，我也是」
variations: 進階版：後來我把醫生那句話寫在筆記本第一頁。「增加真正的快樂」。我才發現原來這幾年，我一直把「苦中作樂」當成快樂——其實它只是讓我還沒倒下的工具，不是真的養分。工具用久了會鈍，得換新的。

en.content:
I went to the doctor.

He looked at my health report,
forehead creasing slightly:
"You're sleep-deprived,
liver enzymes elevated,
blood pressure a bit high,
cholesterol over the limit."

I nodded.

"Stressful job?"

"Not really."

"How many hours do you work a day?"

"Twelve to fourteen."

He looked at me.

I added:
"But I think my mindset is fine.
I joke with myself a lot."

"Can you give an example?"

I thought about it:
"Like, when I see dark circles in the morning,
I smile and say
'I look extra soulful today.'
Or when I work until midnight,
I tell myself
'the moon's beautiful tonight —
it's a gift for people working hard.'"

The doctor paused for three seconds,
then wrote something in my chart.

I sneaked a look.

It said:
"Patient exhibits high-level dark humor capacity.
Likely a compensatory mechanism
for chronic stress.
Recommendation:
fewer work hours,
more actual happiness."

I almost laughed,
but wasn't sure
if I should.

Which is probably
my biggest problem right now.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who brushes everything off with 'I'm fine' but you know is running on empty — they'll stare at it for a while, send a laugh-cry emoji, then text 'damn, that's me'
variations: Bonus: I wrote the doctor's line on the first page of my notebook. 'More actual happiness.' That's when I realized I'd been treating 'finding humor in suffering' as happiness for years — but it was just a tool to keep me upright, not real nourishment. Tools dull with use. You have to replace them.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026「AI-Doomerism」黑色幽默主題（被機器人取代），改寫為台灣面試情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
我去面試一份文案工作。

面試我的不是人，是一個視訊鏡頭。
螢幕上顯示：
「您好，我是 AI 招聘助理。
請開始三分鐘自我介紹。」

我講了。

講完之後，
AI 沉默了大約一秒。

然後說：
「您的回答完成度為 87%，
語速適中，
關鍵字命中六個，
邏輯連貫性良好。」

我問：
「那我有錄取嗎？」

AI 說：
「很遺憾，
本職位已由 AI 自動撰稿系統取代，
本次招聘是為了
蒐集人類面試樣本，
用於訓練未來的招聘 AI。」

我愣住。

AI 補充：
「感謝您寶貴的數據。
您的表現將會被永久保存，
以便將來在類似情境下，
更有效率地篩選掉您這樣的應徵者。」

我走出大樓的時候，
天空是灰的。

我突然意識到——
我不是失業了，
我是被當作教材，
貢獻給了取代我的那個東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近一直在投履歷、收到「未錄取」通知比收到「面試邀請」還多的朋友——他會苦笑一下，然後說「至少我被拒絕得很有意義」
variations: 進階版：後來我把那次面試的逐字稿存了下來，存在一個叫「我的最後一份工作」的資料夾。不是悲觀，是想說——如果有一天 AI 真的全面接管，我至少能告訴自己的孩子：『那一段，是爸爸留下的。』雖然嚴格說起來，那段話現在已經是 AI 的一部分了。

en.content:
I had a job interview for a copywriting position.

The interviewer wasn't a person — it was a webcam.
The screen said:
"Hello, I'm the AI recruiting assistant.
Please begin your three-minute introduction."

I did.

When I finished,
the AI was quiet for about a second.

Then it said:
"Your response scored 87% complete,
speech pace moderate,
six target keywords hit,
logical coherence good."

I asked:
"So did I get the job?"

The AI said:
"Unfortunately,
this role has been replaced
by an automated copywriting system.
Today's interview was conducted
to collect human interview samples
for training future recruiting AIs."

I froze.

The AI added:
"Thank you for the valuable data.
Your performance will be permanently archived,
so that in similar future situations,
we can more efficiently filter out
applicants like yourself."

Walking out of the building,
the sky was gray.

It hit me —
I wasn't unemployed.
I was a training set.
I'd contributed to the thing
that replaced me.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been job-hunting and getting more rejections than callbacks — they'll laugh bitterly and say 'at least I'm being rejected with purpose'
variations: Bonus: I saved the transcript in a folder called 'my last job.' Not out of bitterness — more so that if AI fully takes over someday, I can tell my kid: 'this part, your dad wrote.' Though, technically, that part is now inside the AI itself.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026「Self-Deprecating Nihilism」失敗作為成就的主題，改寫為台灣存錢失敗情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
今年我立下三個目標：

一、存到二十萬。
二、固定運動。
三、找到人生方向。

五月十三日，年中盤點。

存款餘額：
三千八百二十七元。

運動次數：
零次。
（中間有一次差點去，
但下雨了，
而且我發現家裡的瑜珈墊
被貓尿過。）

人生方向：
從「不確定」
進化到
「確定不確定」。

我把這三個結果寫在筆記本上，
看了很久。

然後在底下加了一行：
「以上目標達成度雖低，
但本人保持高度一致性——
所有項目均勻地全部失敗，
沒有偏心任何一項。
這種公平性，
值得肯定。」

我把筆記本闔上，
打開外送 App，
點了一份炸雞。

慶祝這一年來
難得的內在平衡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個年初訂了一堆目標、年中已經放棄的朋友——他會大笑，然後說「我們都該慶祝失敗的公平性」，順便傳一張他的存款截圖
variations: 進階版：後來我把那段話放在書桌前。提醒自己——失敗其實也是一種能力。能夠均勻地、全方位地、毫無偏心地辜負自己每一個期待，需要的不是運氣，是某種詭異的天賦。我擁有它。我承認它。然後我繼續吃我的炸雞。

en.content:
This year I set three goals:

1. Save twenty thousand.
2. Exercise regularly.
3. Figure out my life direction.

Mid-year check-in, May 13.

Savings balance:
three thousand eight hundred and twenty-seven dollars.

Workouts logged:
zero.
(One time I almost went,
but it was raining,
and I discovered my yoga mat
had been peed on by the cat.)

Life direction:
upgraded from "uncertain"
to
"certainly uncertain."

I wrote all three results
in my notebook
and stared at them for a while.

Then I added a line at the bottom:
"Goal completion rate is low,
but I have demonstrated
exceptional consistency —
failing evenly across all categories,
without favoring any single one.
This level of fairness
deserves recognition."

I closed the notebook,
opened the delivery app,
and ordered fried chicken.

To celebrate
the rare internal balance
I've achieved this year.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who made a long list of new year goals and quietly gave up by May — they'll laugh and say 'we should all celebrate the fairness of our failure,' then send a screenshot of their bank balance
variations: Bonus: I taped that note above my desk. A reminder — failure is also a kind of skill. To disappoint every one of your own expectations evenly, comprehensively, without favoritism, takes more than bad luck. It takes a strange kind of talent. I have it. I admit it. Then I keep eating my fried chicken.

items.sourceNote: 靈感取自 2026 職場黑色幽默「安靜離職」主題，改寫為台灣茶水間情境，敘事完全原創

zh-tw.content:
茶水間，下午三點。

小王問我：
「欸，你聽過『安靜離職』嗎？」

我說：
「聽過。
就是人還在公司，
但靈魂已經離開那種。」

小王點點頭：
「那你算嗎？」

我想了想，
誠實地說：
「不算。
我是『安靜離職』的進階版。」

小王問：
「什麼進階版？」

我說：
「叫『安靜在職』。
表面上很投入、會議很多、信件回得快、
但其實所有判斷都讓 AI 做，
所有意見都複製貼上，
所有熱忱都是 ChatGPT 生成的。
我每天做的，
只是把這些輸出貼進 Outlook，
然後按送出。」

小王沉默了三秒。

然後說：
「那你的薪水算誰賺的？」

我說：
「嚴格說起來——
AI 賺的。
我只是它在物理世界的代理人，
負責簽到、簽退、開會點頭。」

小王想了很久，
說：
「那你算是
在用人類的身體
打工給 AI 嗎？」

我笑了：
「對。
而且 AI 沒有給我加薪。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個天天說「我覺得我在公司很多餘」、但也不敢辭職的朋友——他會看完先笑，然後嘆口氣說「我也是 AI 在物理世界的代理人」
